revision.text:
'''押媛（''おしひめ'''）は、[[孝安天皇]]の[[皇后]]。別名は長媛または五十坂媛。[[葉江]]または[[五十坂彦]]の娘。[[孝霊天皇]]と[[大吉備諸進命]]の生母。
== 関連項目 == *[[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]]
== 関連史料 == *坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代人名辭典』(吉川弘文館) *『天皇家系譜総覧』(新人物往来社)

revision.comment: +cat
revision.text:
'''押媛'''（おしひめ）は、[[孝安天皇]]の[[皇后]]。別名は長媛または五十坂媛。[[葉江]]または[[五十坂彦]]の娘。[[孝霊天皇]]と[[大吉備諸進命]]の生母。
== 関連項目 == * [[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]]
== 関連史料 == * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代人名辭典』（吉川弘文館） * 『天皇家系譜総覧』（新人物往来社）
{{DEFAULTSORT:おしひめ}} [[Category:日本神話の人物]] [[Category:古墳時代以前の皇族]] [[Category:日本の皇后]]

revision.comment: 天皇略系図、修正
revision.text:
[[Image:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|thumb|right|400px|天皇略系図（初代 - 第10代） [[media:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|SVGで表示（対応ブラウザのみ）]]]] '''押媛'''（おしひめ）は、[[欠史八代]]、[[孝安天皇]]の[[皇后]]。[[孝霊天皇]]と[[大吉備諸進命]]の生母。
『日本書紀』本文の皇后で天足彦國押人の娘かつ孝安天皇の姪である。『日本書紀』第1の一書での皇后は磯城県主葉江の娘である長媛、第2の一書での皇后は十市県主五十坂彦の娘である五十坂媛。『古事記』の皇后は孝安天皇の姪の忍鹿比売。
== 関連項目 == * [[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]]
== 関連史料 == * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代人名辭典』（吉川弘文館） * 『天皇家系譜総覧』（新人物往来社）
{{DEFAULTSORT:おしひめ}} [[Category:日本神話の人物]] [[Category:古墳時代以前の皇族]] [[Category:日本の皇后]]

[[Image:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|thumb|right|400px|天皇略系図（初代 - 第10代） [[media:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|SVGで表示（対応ブラウザのみ）]]]] '''押媛'''（おしひめ）は、[[欠史八代]]、[[孝安天皇]]の[[皇后]]。[[孝霊天皇]]と[[大吉備諸進命]]の生母。
『日本書紀』本文の皇后で天足彦國押人の娘かつ孝安天皇の姪である。『日本書紀』第1の一書での皇后は磯城県主葉江の娘である長媛、第2の一書での皇后は十市県主五十坂彦の娘である五十坂媛。『古事記』の皇后は孝安天皇の姪の忍鹿比売。
== 関連項目 == * [[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]]
== 関連史料 == * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代人名辭典』（吉川弘文館） * 『天皇家系譜総覧』（新人物往来社）
{{DEFAULTSORT:おしひめ}} [[Category:日本神話の人物]] [[Category:古墳時代以前の皇族]] [[Category:日本の皇后]] [[zh:押媛]]

第 2皇子：'''[[孝霊天皇]]'''（第 7代[[天皇]]） | 王家 = | 王朝 = | 王室歌 = | 父親 = 不明 | 母親 = 不明 | 宗教 = | サイン = }} '''押媛'''（おしひめ）は、[[欠史八代]]、[[孝安天皇]]の[[皇后]]。[[孝霊天皇]]と[[大吉備諸進命]]の生母。 『日本書紀』本文の皇后で天足彦國押人の娘かつ孝安天皇の姪である。『日本書紀』第1の一書での皇后は磯城県主葉江の娘である長媛、第2の一書での皇后は十市県主五十坂彦の娘である五十坂媛。『古事記』の皇后は孝安天皇の姪の忍鹿比売。 __TOC__ {{-}} == 関連項目 == [[Image:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|thumb|right|400px|天皇略系図（初代 - 第10代） [[media:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|SVGで表示（対応ブラウザのみ）]]]] * [[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]] == 関連史料 == * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代人名辭典』（[[吉川弘文館]]） * 『天皇家系譜総覧』（[[新人物往来社]]） {{DEFAULTSORT:おしひめ}} [[Category:日本神話の人物]] [[Category:古墳時代以前の皇族]] [[Category:日本の皇后]] [[zh:押媛]]

revision.comment: 参考
revision.text:
{{基礎情報 君主 | 人名 = 押媛
（おしひめ） | 各国語表記 = | 君主号 = [[日本の皇后一覧|日本の皇后]] | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 在位 = [[紀元前392年]][[3月3日]] - [[紀元前291年]][[2月27日]] | 戴冠日 = | 別号 = | 全名 = | 出生日 = 不明 | 生地 = 不明 | 死亡日 = 不明 | 没地 = 不明 | 埋葬日 = 不明 | 埋葬地 = 不明 | 継承者 = | 継承形式 = | 配偶者1 = [[孝安天皇]]（第 6代[[天皇]]） | 配偶者2 = | 配偶者3 = | 配偶者4 = | 配偶者5 = | 配偶者6 = | 配偶者7 = | 配偶者8 = | 配偶者9 = | 配偶者10 = | 子女 = 第 1[[皇子]]：[[大吉備諸進命]]
第 2皇子：'''[[孝霊天皇]]'''（第 7代[[天皇]]） | 王家 = | 王朝 = | 王室歌 = | 父親 = 不明 | 母親 = 不明 | 宗教 = | サイン = }} [[Image:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|thumb|right|400px|天皇略系図（初代 - 第10代） [[media:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|SVGで表示（対応ブラウザのみ）]]]] '''押媛'''（おしひめ）は、[[欠史八代]]、[[孝安天皇]]の[[皇后]]。[[孝霊天皇]]と[[大吉備諸進命]]の生母。 『日本書紀』本文の皇后で天足彦國押人の娘かつ孝安天皇の姪である。『日本書紀』第1の一書での皇后は磯城県主葉江の娘である長媛、第2の一書での皇后は十市県主五十坂彦の娘である五十坂媛。『古事記』の皇后は孝安天皇の姪の忍鹿比売。 == 参考 == [[富士山本宮浅間大社|駿河浅間大社]]の大宮司家（[[富士氏]]）は和爾部姓で和邇系図（[[和珥氏]]を参照）を伝えており、真偽はつまびらかでないが、[[孝昭天皇]]の皇子[[天足彦国押人命]]の子として「押媛命（一に忍鹿比売命、母は建田背命の妹 宇那比媛命也。孝安天皇の皇后）弟 和爾日子押人命（一に稚押彦命、母同上。大倭和邇里に居る）」と記載されている太田亮『姓氏家系大辞典』角川書店（1963年）第三巻P6661より。。 == 脚注 == == 関連史料 == * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代人名辭典』（[[吉川弘文館]]） * 『天皇家系譜総覧』（[[新人物往来社]]） == 関連項目 == * [[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]] {{DEFAULTSORT:おしひめ}} [[Category:日本神話の人物]] [[Category:古墳時代以前の皇族]] [[Category:日本の皇后]] [[zh:押媛]]

（おしひめ） | 各国語表記 = | 君主号 = [[日本の皇后一覧|日本の皇后]] | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 在位 = [[紀元前392年]][[3月3日]] - [[紀元前291年]][[2月27日]] | 戴冠日 = | 別号 = | 全名 = | 出生日 = 不明 | 生地 = 不明 | 死亡日 = 不明 | 没地 = 不明 | 埋葬日 = 不明 | 埋葬地 = 不明 | 継承者 = | 継承形式 = | 配偶者1 = [[孝安天皇]]（第 6代[[天皇]]） | 配偶者2 = | 配偶者3 = | 配偶者4 = | 配偶者5 = | 配偶者6 = | 配偶者7 = | 配偶者8 = | 配偶者9 = | 配偶者10 = | 子女 = 第 1[[皇子]]：[[大吉備諸進命]]
第 2皇子：'''[[孝霊天皇]]'''（第 7代[[天皇]]） | 王家 = | 王朝 = | 王室歌 = | 父親 = 不明 | 母親 = 不明 | 宗教 = | サイン = }} [[Image:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|thumb|right|400px|天皇略系図（初代 - 第10代） [[media:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|SVGで表示（対応ブラウザのみ）]]]] '''押媛'''（おしひめ）は、[[欠史八代]]、[[孝安天皇]]の[[皇后]]。[[孝霊天皇]]と[[大吉備諸進命]]の生母。 『日本書紀』本文の皇后で[[天足彦国押人命|天足彦國押人]]の娘かつ孝安天皇の姪である。『日本書紀』第1の一書での皇后は磯城県主葉江の娘である長媛、第2の一書での皇后は十市県主五十坂彦の娘である五十坂媛。『古事記』の皇后は孝安天皇の姪の忍鹿比売。 == 参考 == [[富士山本宮浅間大社|駿河浅間大社]]の大宮司家（[[富士氏]]）は和爾部姓で和邇系図（[[和珥氏]]を参照）を伝えており、真偽はつまびらかでないが、[[孝昭天皇]]の皇子[[天足彦国押人命]]の子として「押媛命（一に忍鹿比売命、母は建田背命の妹 宇那比媛命也。孝安天皇の皇后）弟 和爾日子押人命（一に稚押彦命、母同上。大倭和邇里に居る）」と記載されている[[太田亮]]『姓氏家系大辞典』[[角川書店]]（1963年）第三巻P6661より。。 == 脚注 == == 関連史料 == * [[坂本太郎 (歴史学者)|坂本太郎]]・平野邦雄『日本古代人名辭典』（[[吉川弘文館]]） * 『天皇家系譜総覧』（[[新人物往来社]]） == 関連項目 == * [[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]] {{歴代皇后一覧}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おしひめ}} [[Category:日本神話の人物]] [[Category:古墳時代以前の皇族]] [[Category:日本の皇后]]

[[孝霊天皇]] |父親=[[天足彦国押人命]] |母親= |役職= }} [[ファイル:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|thumb|right|400px|天皇略系図（初代 - 第10代） [[media:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|SVGで表示（対応ブラウザのみ）]]]] '''押媛'''（おしひめ）は、[[欠史八代]]、[[孝安天皇]]の[[皇后]]。[[孝霊天皇]]と[[大吉備諸進命]]の生母。 『日本書紀』本文の皇后で[[天足彦国押人命|天足彦国押人]]の娘かつ孝安天皇の姪である。『日本書紀』第1の一書での皇后は磯城県主葉江の娘である長媛、第2の一書での皇后は十市県主五十坂彦の娘である五十坂媛。『古事記』の皇后は孝安天皇の姪の忍鹿比売。 == 参考 == [[富士山本宮浅間大社|駿河浅間大社]]の大宮司家（[[富士氏]]）は和爾部姓で和邇系図（[[和珥氏]]を参照）を伝えており、真偽は詳らかでないが、[[孝昭天皇]]の皇子[[天足彦国押人命]]の子として「押媛命（一に忍鹿比売命、母は建田背命の妹 宇那比媛命也。孝安天皇の皇后）弟 和爾日子押人命（一に稚押彦命、母同上。大倭和邇里に居る）」と記載されている[[太田亮]]『姓氏家系大辞典』[[角川書店]]（1963年）第三巻P6661より。。 == 脚注 == {{reflist}} == 関連史料 == * [[坂本太郎 (歴史学者)|坂本太郎]]・[[平野邦雄]]『日本古代人名辭典』（[[吉川弘文館]]） * 『天皇家系譜総覧』（[[新人物往来社]]） {{歴代皇后一覧}} {{歴代日本の皇太后一覧}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おしひめ}} [[Category:日本の皇后]] [[Category:日本の皇太后]] [[Category:古墳時代以前の女性皇族]]

{{基礎情報 皇后 |名 = 押媛 |立后根拠 = 第6代天皇后 |画像 = |画像サイズ = |画像説明 = |画像代替文 = |在位期間 = |和暦在位期間 = |皇后 = 孝安天皇26年2月14日 |皇太后 = 孝霊天皇元年1月12日 |出生日 = |生地 = |死亡日 = |没地 = |陵所 = |諱 = |諡号 = |異称 = |幼称 = |氏族 = [[和邇氏]] |父親 = [[天足彦国押人命]] |母親 = |配偶者1 = [[孝安天皇]] |結婚 = |子女 =[[大吉備諸進命]]
[[孝霊天皇]] |養子女 = |皇居 = |空欄表題1 = 異表記 |空欄記載1 = 忍鹿比売命（古事記）
押媛命（先代旧事本紀） |空欄表題2 = 異説 |空欄記載2 = 磯城県主[[葉江]]の娘・長媛
十市県主五十坂彦の娘・五十坂媛 |脚注 = }} '''押媛'''（おしひめ）は古代日本の人物。 == 概要 == 異表記として'''忍鹿比売命'''（おしかひめのみこと）、押媛命（おしひめのみこと）などが存在する。 『[[古事記]]』、『[[日本書紀]]』、『[[先代旧事本紀]]』など各史料に登場する女性で、[[孝安天皇]]の[[皇后]]として[[孝霊天皇]]と[[大吉備諸進命]]を生む。[[天足彦国押人命|天足彦国押人]]の娘かつ孝安天皇の姪とされる。 ただし『日本書紀』第1の一書での孝安天皇の皇后は、磯城県主[[葉江]]の娘である[[長媛]]で、第2の一書では十市県主五十坂彦の娘である五十坂媛とされている。 [[ファイル:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|thumb|right|400px|天皇略系図（初代 - 第10代） [[media:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|SVGで表示（対応ブラウザのみ）]]]] == 参考 == [[富士山本宮浅間大社|駿河浅間大社]]の大宮司家（[[富士氏]]）は和爾部姓で和邇系図（[[和珥氏]]を参照）を伝えており、真偽は詳らかでないが、[[孝昭天皇]]の皇子[[天足彦国押人命]]の子として「押媛命（一に忍鹿比売命、母は建田背命の妹 宇那比媛命也。孝安天皇の皇后）弟 和爾日子押人命（一に稚押彦命、母同上。大倭和邇里に居る）」と記載されている[[太田亮]]『姓氏家系大辞典』[[角川書店]]（1963年）第三巻P6661より。。 == 脚注 == {{reflist}} == 関連史料 == * [[坂本太郎 (歴史学者)|坂本太郎]]・[[平野邦雄]]『日本古代人名辭典』（[[吉川弘文館]]） * 『天皇家系譜総覧』（[[新人物往来社]]） {{歴代皇后一覧}} {{歴代日本の皇太后一覧}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おしひめ}} [[Category:日本の皇后]] [[Category:日本の皇太后]] [[Category:古墳時代以前の女性皇族]]

十市県主五十坂彦の娘・五十坂媛 |脚注 = }} '''押媛'''（おしひめ）は古代日本の人物。 == 概要 == 異表記として'''忍鹿比売命'''（おしかひめのみこと）、押媛命（おしひめのみこと）などが存在する。 『[[古事記]]』、『[[日本書紀]]』、『[[先代旧事本紀]]』など各史料に登場する女性で、[[孝安天皇]]の[[皇后]]として[[孝霊天皇]]と[[大吉備諸進命]]を生む。[[天足彦国押人命|天足彦国押人]]の娘かつ孝安天皇の姪とされる。 ただし『日本書紀』第1の一書での孝安天皇の皇后は、磯城県主[[葉江]]の娘である[[長媛]]で、第2の一書では十市県主五十坂彦の娘である五十坂媛とされている。 [[ファイル:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|thumb|right|400px|天皇略系図（初代 - 第10代） [[media:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|SVGで表示（対応ブラウザのみ）]]]] == 参考 == [[富士山本宮浅間大社|駿河浅間大社]]の大宮司家（[[富士氏]]）は和爾部姓で和邇系図（[[和珥氏]]を参照）を伝えており、真偽は詳らかでないが、[[孝昭天皇]]の皇子[[天足彦国押人命]]の子として「押媛命（一に忍鹿比売命、母は建田背命の妹 宇那比媛命也。孝安天皇の皇后）弟 和爾日子押人命（一に稚押彦命、母同上。大倭和邇里に居る）」と記載されている[[太田亮]]『姓氏家系大辞典』[[角川書店]]（1963年）第三巻P6661より。。 == 脚注 == {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == *[[坂本太郎 (歴史学者)|坂本太郎]]・[[平野邦雄]]『日本古代人名辭典』（[[吉川弘文館]]） *『天皇家系譜総覧』（[[新人物往来社]]） *[[太田亮]]『姓氏家系大辞典』（[[角川書店]]） == 関連項目 == *[[欠史八代]] {{歴代皇后一覧}} {{歴代日本の皇太后一覧}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おしひめ}} [[Category:日本の皇后]] [[Category:日本の皇太后]] [[Category:古墳時代以前の女性皇族]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

{{Infobox Empress | name = Oshihime | imperial_consortrank = Empress of the 6th Emperor | image = | image_size = | caption = | alternative_text = | reign = | era_reign = | consort = February 14, 26th year of [[Emperor Kōan]]'s reign | empress_dowager = January 12, 1st year of [[Emperor Kōrei]]'s reign | birth_date = | birth_place = | death_date = | death_place = | burial_place = | posthumous_name = | alternate_titles = [[Ninokahime no Mikoto]] (忍鹿比売命)
Oshihime no Mikoto (押媛命) | childhood_name = | clan = [[Wani clan]] | father = [[Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto]] | mother = | spouse = [[Emperor Kōan]] | marriage = | children = [[Ōkibi no Morosusumi no Mikoto]]
[[Emperor Kōrei]] | adopted_children = | imperial_residence = | blank_label1 = Alternate Names | blank_data1 = Ninokahime no Mikoto (''Kojiki'')
Oshihime no Mikoto (''Sendai Kuji Hongi'') | blank_label2 = Alternate Theories | blank_data2 = Daughter of [[Hae]], head of [[Shiki Province]] (長媛)
Daughter of Isaka Hikono of [[Tōchi Province]] (五十坂媛) | notes = }} '''Oshihime''' (押媛) is a figure from ancient [[Japan]], appearing in multiple historical sources. == Overview == Oshihime is alternatively known as '''Ninokahime no Mikoto''' (忍鹿比売命) or '''Oshihime no Mikoto''' (押媛命) in historical texts such as the ''[[Kojiki]]'', ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', and ''[[Sendai Kuji Hongi]]''. She was the consort of [[Emperor Kōan]] and the mother of [[Emperor Kōrei]] and [[Ōkibi no Morosusumi no Mikoto]]. Oshihime is described as the daughter of [[Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto]] and is thus Emperor Kōan’s niece. The ''Nihon Shoki'' offers alternative accounts of Emperor Kōan’s consort. In the first supplementary narrative, she is identified as [[Nagahime]], daughter of [[Hae]] of Shiki Province. In the second, she is [[Isaka Hime]], daughter of Isaka Hikono of Tōchi Province. [[File:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|thumb|right|400px|Simplified genealogy of the Japanese emperors (1st–10th). [[media:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|View in SVG (supported browsers only)]]]] == References == The [[Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha|Suruga Sengen Taisha]] lineage claims descent from the [[Wani clan]], as recorded in a genealogical chart (see [[Wani clan]]). This chart attributes Oshihime as the daughter of [[Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto]], alongside her brother Wani Hikoono no Mikoto. However, the historian [[Takakuni Higo]] has argued that this genealogy was likely fabricated in later centuries.[[Takakuni Higo]], "Formation and Authenticity of Lineage Charts: The Case of the Wani Genealogy in Suruga Sengen Shrine," ''Japanese Ancient History Essays,'' 1988. == Notes == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * [[Tarō Sakamoto (historian)|Tarō Sakamoto]] and [[Kunio Hirano]], ''Japanese Ancient Personal Names Dictionary'' ([[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]) * ''Complete Genealogy of the Imperial Family'' (Shinjinbutsu Ōraisha) * [[Ryō Ōta]], ''Dictionary of Surnames and Genealogy'' ([[Kadokawa Shoten]]) == Related Articles == * [[Kesshi Hachidai]] * [[List of individuals before the Asuka period]] {{List of Empresses of Japan}} {{List of Empress Dowagers of Japan}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Oshihime}} [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Empresses of Japan]] [[Category:Empress Dowagers of Japan]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese figures]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] ==Genealogy== {{User:Immanuelle/Generations of Watatsumi}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:dd]]'''Oshihime''' is a relative of early Japanese Emperors. See the family tree below for more info. ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:Generations of Jimmu that need correction]] [[Category:Dd]][[ja:押媛]] ]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox Empress | name = Oshihime | imperial_consortrank = Empress of the 6th Emperor | image = | image_size = | caption = | alternative_text = | reign = | era_reign = | consort = February 14, 26th year of [[Emperor Kōan]]'s reign | empress_dowager = January 12, 1st year of [[Emperor Kōrei]]'s reign | birth_date = | birth_place = | death_date = | death_place = | burial_place = | posthumous_name = | alternate_titles = [[Ninokahime no Mikoto]] (忍鹿比売命)
Oshihime no Mikoto (押媛命) | childhood_name = | clan = [[Wani clan]] | father = [[Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto]] | mother = | spouse = [[Emperor Kōan]] | marriage = | children = [[Ōkibi no Morosusumi no Mikoto]]
[[Emperor Kōrei]] | adopted_children = | imperial_residence = | blank_label1 = Alternate Names | blank_data1 = Ninokahime no Mikoto (''Kojiki'')
Oshihime no Mikoto (''Sendai Kuji Hongi'') | blank_label2 = Alternate Theories | blank_data2 = Daughter of [[Hae]], head of [[Shiki Province]] (長媛)
Daughter of Isaka Hikono of [[Tōchi Province]] (五十坂媛) | notes = }} '''Oshihime''' (押媛) is a figure from ancient [[Japan]], appearing in multiple historical sources. == Overview == Oshihime is alternatively known as '''Ninokahime no Mikoto''' (忍鹿比売命) or '''Oshihime no Mikoto''' (押媛命) in historical texts such as the ''[[Kojiki]]'', ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', and ''[[Sendai Kuji Hongi]]''. She was the consort of [[Emperor Kōan]] and the mother of [[Emperor Kōrei]] and [[Ōkibi no Morosusumi no Mikoto]]. Oshihime is described as the daughter of [[Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto]] and is thus Emperor Kōan’s niece. The ''Nihon Shoki'' offers alternative accounts of Emperor Kōan’s consort. In the first supplementary narrative, she is identified as [[Nagahime]], daughter of [[Hae]] of Shiki Province. In the second, she is [[Isaka Hime]], daughter of Isaka Hikono of Tōchi Province. [[File:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|thumb|right|400px|Simplified genealogy of the Japanese emperors (1st–10th). [[Media:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|View in SVG (supported browsers only)]]]] == References == The [[Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha|Suruga Sengen Taisha]] lineage claims descent from the [[Wani clan]], as recorded in a genealogical chart (see [[Wani clan]]). This chart attributes Oshihime as the daughter of [[Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto]], alongside her brother Wani Hikoono no Mikoto. However, the historian [[Takakuni Higo]] has argued that this genealogy was likely fabricated in later centuries.[[Takakuni Higo]], "Formation and Authenticity of Lineage Charts: The Case of the Wani Genealogy in Suruga Sengen Shrine," ''Japanese Ancient History Essays,'' 1988. == Notes == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * [[Tarō Sakamoto (historian)|Tarō Sakamoto]] and [[Kunio Hirano]], ''Japanese Ancient Personal Names Dictionary'' ([[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]) * ''Complete Genealogy of the Imperial Family'' (Shinjinbutsu Ōraisha) * [[Ryō Ōta]], ''Dictionary of Surnames and Genealogy'' ([[Kadokawa Shoten]]) == Related Articles == * [[Kesshi Hachidai]] * [[List of individuals before the Asuka period]] {{List of Empresses of Japan}} {{List of Empress Dowagers of Japan}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Oshihime}} [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== '''Oshihime''' is a relative of early Japanese Emperors. See the family tree below for more info. ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:Generations of Jimmu that need correction]] [[ja:押媛]] ]]

Daughter of Isaka Hikono of [[Tōchi Province]] (五十坂媛) | notes = }} '''Oshihime''' (押媛) is a figure from ancient [[Japan]], appearing in multiple historical sources. == Overview == Oshihime is alternatively known as '''Ninokahime no Mikoto''' (忍鹿比売命) or '''Oshihime no Mikoto''' (押媛命) in historical texts such as the ''[[Kojiki]]'', ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', and ''[[Sendai Kuji Hongi]]''. She was the consort of [[Emperor Kōan]] and the mother of [[Emperor Kōrei]] and [[Ōkibi no Morosusumi no Mikoto]]. Oshihime is described as the daughter of [[Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto]] and is thus Emperor Kōan’s niece. The ''Nihon Shoki'' offers alternative accounts of Emperor Kōan’s consort. In the first supplementary narrative, she is identified as [[Nagahime]], daughter of [[Hae]] of Shiki Province. In the second, she is [[Isaka Hime]], daughter of Isaka Hikono of Tōchi Province. [[File:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|thumb|right|400px|Simplified genealogy of the Japanese emperors (1st–10th). [[Media:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|View in SVG (supported browsers only)]]]] == References == The [[Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha|Suruga Sengen Taisha]] lineage claims descent from the [[Wani clan]], as recorded in a genealogical chart (see [[Wani clan]]). This chart attributes Oshihime as the daughter of [[Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto]], alongside her brother Wani Hikoono no Mikoto. However, the historian [[Takakuni Higo]] has argued that this genealogy was likely fabricated in later centuries.[[Takakuni Higo]], "Formation and Authenticity of Lineage Charts: The Case of the Wani Genealogy in Suruga Sengen Shrine," ''Japanese Ancient History Essays,'' 1988. == Notes == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * [[Tarō Sakamoto (historian)|Tarō Sakamoto]] and [[Kunio Hirano]], ''Japanese Ancient Personal Names Dictionary'' ([[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]) * ''Complete Genealogy of the Imperial Family'' (Shinjinbutsu Ōraisha) * [[Ryō Ōta]], ''Dictionary of Surnames and Genealogy'' ([[Kadokawa Shoten]]) == Related Articles == * [[Kesshi Hachidai]] * [[List of individuals before the Asuka period]] {{List of Empresses of Japan}} {{List of Empress Dowagers of Japan}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Oshihime}} [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:pages from wikipedia]][[en:Oshihime]][[Category:pages from wikipedia]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== '''Oshihime''' is a relative of early Japanese Emperors. See the family tree below for more info. ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:Generations of Jimmu that need correction]] [[Category:original pages]][[simple:Oshihime]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Original pages]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Oshihime]][[category:original pages]] [[en:Oshihime]][[ja:押媛]] ]]

[[Emperor Kōrei]] | adopted_children = | imperial_residence = | blank_label1 = Alternate Names | blank_data1 = Ninokahime no Mikoto (''Kojiki'')
Oshihime no Mikoto (''Sendai Kuji Hongi'') | blank_label2 = Alternate Theories | blank_data2 = Daughter of [[Hae]], head of [[Shiki Province]] (長媛)
Daughter of Isaka Hikono of [[Tōchi Province]] (五十坂媛) | notes = }} '''Oshihime''' (押媛) is a figure from ancient [[Japan]], appearing in multiple historical sources. == Overview == Oshihime is alternatively known as '''Ninokahime no Mikoto''' (忍鹿比売命) or '''Oshihime no Mikoto''' (押媛命) in historical texts such as the ''[[Kojiki]]'', ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', and ''[[Sendai Kuji Hongi]]''. She was the consort of [[Emperor Kōan]] and the mother of [[Emperor Kōrei]] and [[Ōkibi no Morosusumi no Mikoto]]. Oshihime is described as the daughter of [[Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto]] and is thus Emperor Kōan’s niece. The ''Nihon Shoki'' offers alternative accounts of Emperor Kōan’s consort. In the first supplementary narrative, she is identified as [[Nagahime]], daughter of [[Hae]] of Shiki Province. In the second, she is [[Isaka Hime]], daughter of Isaka Hikono of Tōchi Province. [[File:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|thumb|right|400px|Simplified genealogy of the Japanese emperors (1st–10th). [[Media:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|View in SVG (supported browsers only)]]]] == References == The [[Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha|Suruga Sengen Taisha]] lineage claims descent from the [[Wani clan]], as recorded in a genealogical chart (see [[Wani clan]]). This chart attributes Oshihime as the daughter of [[Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto]], alongside her brother Wani Hikoono no Mikoto. However, the historian [[Takakuni Higo]] has argued that this genealogy was likely fabricated in later centuries.[[Takakuni Higo]], "Formation and Authenticity of Lineage Charts: The Case of the Wani Genealogy in Suruga Sengen Shrine," ''Japanese Ancient History Essays,'' 1988. == Notes == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * [[Tarō Sakamoto (historian)|Tarō Sakamoto]] and [[Kunio Hirano]], ''Japanese Ancient Personal Names Dictionary'' ([[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]) * ''Complete Genealogy of the Imperial Family'' (Shinjinbutsu Ōraisha) * [[Ryō Ōta]], ''Dictionary of Surnames and Genealogy'' ([[Kadokawa Shoten]]) == Related Articles == * [[Kesshi Hachidai]] * [[List of individuals before the Asuka period]] {{List of Empresses of Japan}} {{List of Empress Dowagers of Japan}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Oshihime}} [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[en:Oshihime]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== '''Oshihime''' is a relative of early Japanese Emperors. See the family tree below for more info. ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:Generations of Jimmu that need correction]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Original pages]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Oshihime]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:Original pages]]{{Consorts of Japan}}[[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:dd]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Oshihime]][[category:dd]] [[Category:dd]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Oshihime]][[category:dd]] [[en:Oshihime]][[ja:押媛]] ]]

{{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}} | other_titles = Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto (忍鹿比売命)
Oshihime no Mikoto (押媛命) | other_theories = Daughter of {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi Hae|ja|磯城県主葉江}}, named Nagahime
Daughter of {{ill|Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko|ja|十市県主五十坂彦}}, named Isosaka-hime }} '''Oshihime''' (押媛) is a figure from ancient Japan. == Overview == Alternate titles include '''Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto''' (忍鹿比売命) and Oshihime no Mikoto (押媛命). She is featured in historical texts such as the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, and {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}. Oshihime is described as the consort of {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}}, mother to {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}} and {{ill|Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|ja|大吉備諸進命}}. She is said to be the daughter of {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命}}, making her both the niece and consort of Emperor Kōan. However, in the first supplementary text of the Nihon Shoki regarding Emperor Kōan, the empress is identified as Nagahime, daughter of Shiki Agatanushi Hae. In the second supplementary text, the empress is named Isosaka-hime, daughter of Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko. [[File:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|thumb|right|400px|Family tree of emperors (1st–10th) [[Media:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|View SVG (browser support required)]]]] == References == The {{ill|Fuji Clan|ja|富士氏}}, who served as grand priests of {{ill|Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha|ja|富士山本宮浅間大社}}, trace their lineage to the Wani clan and preserved the Wani genealogy (see {{ill|Wani Clan|ja|和珥氏}}). According to this genealogy, Oshihime is listed as the daughter of {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命}}, with an alternate name of Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto. She is recorded as Emperor Kōan's empress, while her brother is identified as Wani Hikoshihito no Mikoto. The accuracy of this record remains disputed. {{ill|Higo Ryūkai|ja|比護隆界}} claimed that these genealogies were fabricated in later periods.Higo Ryūkai, "Formation and Reliability of Clan Genealogies: On the 'Wani Clan Genealogy' from Suruga Sengen Shrine," *Japanese Ancient History Essays*, 1988. == Notes == {{reflist}} == Sources == * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō (historian)|ja|坂本太郎}} and Hirano Kunio, *Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Comprehensive Overview of the Imperial Family Lineage* (Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha) * {{ill|Ōta Ryō|ja|太田亮}}, *Dictionary of Surnames and Genealogies* (Kadokawa Shoten) == Related Topics == * {{ill|Eight Unhistorical Monarchs|ja|欠史八代}} * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka Period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} [[Category:Wani Clan]] [[Category:Japanese Empresses]] [[Category:Empress Dowagers of Japan]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Figures]] [[Category:Female Imperial Family Members Before the Kofun Period]] [[Category:Birth and Death Dates Unknown]] [[ja:押媛]] {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Oshihime}} [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[en:Oshihime]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== [[Category:Generations of Jimmu that need correction]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Original pages]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Oshihime]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Oshihime]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Oshihime]][[en:Oshihime]][[ja:押媛]]

The accuracy of this record remains disputed. {{ill|Higo Ryūkai|ja|比護隆界}} claimed that these genealogies were fabricated in later periods.Higo Ryūkai, "Formation and Reliability of Clan Genealogies: On the 'Wani Clan Genealogy' from Suruga Sengen Shrine," *Japanese Ancient History Essays*, 1988. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == Sources == * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō (historian)|ja|坂本太郎}} and Hirano Kunio, *Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Comprehensive Overview of the Imperial Family Lineage* (Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha) * {{ill|Ōta Ryō|ja|太田亮}}, *Dictionary of Surnames and Genealogies* (Kadokawa Shoten) == Related Topics == * {{ill|Eight Unhistorical Monarchs|ja|欠史八代}} * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka Period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} [[Category:Wani Clan]] [[Category:Japanese Empresses]] [[Category:Empress Dowagers of Japan]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Figures]] [[Category:Female Imperial Family Members Before the Kofun Period]] [[Category:Birth and Death Dates Unknown]] [[ja:押媛]] {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Oshihime}} [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[en:Oshihime]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Link incomplete]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Oshihime]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Oshihime]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Oshihime]][[en:Oshihime]][[ja:押媛]]

{{Infobox Empress | name = Oshihime (押媛) | image = | caption = | reign = | era_name_reign = | imperial_consort = Emperor Kōan (孝安天皇) February 14, 26th year of Emperor Kōan | empress_dowager = Emperor Kōrei (孝霊天皇) January 12, 1st year of Emperor Kōrei | birth_date = | birth_place = | death_date = | death_place = | burial_place = | clan = {{ill|Wani Clan|ja|和邇氏}} | father = {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命}} | spouse1 = {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}} | children = {{ill|Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|ja|大吉備諸進命}}

Oshihime no Mikoto (押媛命) | other_theories = Daughter of {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi Hae|ja|磯城県主葉江}}, named Nagahime
Daughter of {{ill|Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko|ja|十市県主五十坂彦}}, named Isosaka-hime }} '''Oshihime''' (押媛) is a figure from ancient Japan. == Overview == Alternate titles include '''Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto''' (忍鹿比売命) and Oshihime no Mikoto (押媛命). She is featured in historical texts such as the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, and {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}. Oshihime is described as the consort of {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}}, mother to {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}} and {{ill|Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|ja|大吉備諸進命}}. She is said to be the daughter of {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命}}, making her both the niece and consort of Emperor Kōan. However, in the first supplementary text of the Nihon Shoki regarding Emperor Kōan, the empress is identified as Nagahime, daughter of Shiki Agatanushi Hae. In the second supplementary text, the empress is named Isosaka-hime, daughter of Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko. [[File:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|thumb|right|400px|Family tree of emperors (1st–10th) [[Media:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|View SVG (browser support required)]]]] == References == The {{ill|Fuji Clan|ja|富士氏}}, who served as grand priests of {{ill|Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha|ja|富士山本宮浅間大社}}, trace their lineage to the Wani clan and preserved the Wani genealogy (see {{ill|Wani Clan|ja|和珥氏}}). According to this genealogy, Oshihime is listed as the daughter of {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命}}, with an alternate name of Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto. She is recorded as Emperor Kōan's empress, while her brother is identified as Wani Hikoshihito no Mikoto. The accuracy of this record remains disputed. {{ill|Higo Ryūkai|ja|比護隆界}} claimed that these genealogies were fabricated in later periods.Higo Ryūkai, "Formation and Reliability of Clan Genealogies: On the 'Wani Clan Genealogy' from Suruga Sengen Shrine," *Japanese Ancient History Essays*, 1988. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == Sources == * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō (historian)|ja|坂本太郎}} and Hirano Kunio, *Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Comprehensive Overview of the Imperial Family Lineage* (Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha) * {{ill|Ōta Ryō|ja|太田亮}}, *Dictionary of Surnames and Genealogies* (Kadokawa Shoten) == Related Topics == * {{ill|Eight Unhistorical Monarchs|ja|欠史八代}} * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka Period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} [[Category:Wani Clan]] [[Category:Japanese Empresses]] [[Category:Empress Dowagers of Japan]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Figures]] [[Category:Female Imperial Family Members Before the Kofun Period]] [[Category:Birth and Death Dates Unknown]] [[ja:押媛]] {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Oshihime}} [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[en:Oshihime]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Link incomplete]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Oshihime]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Oshihime]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Oshihime]][[en:Oshihime]][[ja:押媛]]

The accuracy of this record remains disputed. {{ill|Higo Ryūkai|ja|比護隆界}} claimed that these genealogies were fabricated in later periods.Higo Ryūkai, "Formation and Reliability of Clan Genealogies: On the 'Wani Clan Genealogy' from Suruga Sengen Shrine," *Japanese Ancient History Essays*, 1988. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == Sources == * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō (historian)|ja|坂本太郎}} and Hirano Kunio, *Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Comprehensive Overview of the Imperial Family Lineage* (Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha) * {{ill|Ōta Ryō|ja|太田亮}}, *Dictionary of Surnames and Genealogies* (Kadokawa Shoten) == Related Topics == * {{ill|Eight Unhistorical Monarchs|ja|欠史八代}} * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka Period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} [[Category:Wani Clan]] [[Category:Japanese Empresses]] [[Category:Empress Dowagers of Japan]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Figures]] [[Category:Female Imperial Family Members Before the Kofun Period]] [[Category:Birth and Death Dates Unknown]] [[ja:押媛]] {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Oshihime}} [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[en:Oshihime]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Oshihime]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Oshihime]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Oshihime]][[en:Oshihime]][[ja:押媛]]

{{Infobox Empress | name = Oshihime (押媛) | image = | caption = | reign = | era_name_reign = | imperial_consort = Emperor Kōan (孝安天皇) February 14, 26th year of Emperor Kōan | empress_dowager = Emperor Kōrei (孝霊天皇) January 12, 1st year of Emperor Kōrei | birth_date = | birth_place = | death_date = | death_place = | burial_place = | clan = {{ill|Wani Clan|ja|和邇氏}} | father = {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命}} | spouse1 = {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}} | children = {{ill|Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|ja|大吉備諸進命}}
{{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}} | other_titles = Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto (忍鹿比売命)
Oshihime no Mikoto (押媛命) | other_theories = Daughter of {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi Hae|ja|磯城県主葉江}}, named Nagahime
Daughter of {{ill|Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko|ja|十市県主五十坂彦}}, named Isosaka-hime }} '''Oshihime''' (押媛) is a figure from ancient Japan. == Overview == Alternate titles include '''Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto''' (忍鹿比売命) and Oshihime no Mikoto (押媛命). She is featured in historical texts such as the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, and {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}. Oshihime is described as the consort of {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}}, mother to {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}} and {{ill|Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|ja|大吉備諸進命}}. She is said to be the daughter of {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命}}, making her both the niece and consort of Emperor Kōan. However, in the first supplementary text of the Nihon Shoki regarding Emperor Kōan, the empress is identified as Nagahime, daughter of Shiki Agatanushi Hae. In the second supplementary text, the empress is named Isosaka-hime, daughter of Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko. [[File:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|thumb|right|400px|Family tree of emperors (1st–10th) [[Media:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|View SVG (browser support required)]]]] == References == The {{ill|Fuji Clan|ja|富士氏}}, who served as grand priests of {{ill|Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha|ja|富士山本宮浅間大社}}, trace their lineage to the Wani clan and preserved the Wani genealogy (see {{ill|Wani Clan|ja|和珥氏}}). According to this genealogy, Oshihime is listed as the daughter of {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命}}, with an alternate name of Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto. She is recorded as Emperor Kōan's empress, while her brother is identified as Wani Hikoshihito no Mikoto. The accuracy of this record remains disputed. {{ill|Higo Ryūkai|ja|比護隆界}} claimed that these genealogies were fabricated in later periods.Higo Ryūkai, "Formation and Reliability of Clan Genealogies: On the 'Wani Clan Genealogy' from Suruga Sengen Shrine," *Japanese Ancient History Essays*, 1988. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == Sources == * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō (historian)|ja|坂本太郎}} and Hirano Kunio, *Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Comprehensive Overview of the Imperial Family Lineage* (Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha) * {{ill|Ōta Ryō|ja|太田亮}}, *Dictionary of Surnames and Genealogies* (Kadokawa Shoten) == Related Topics == * {{ill|Eight Unhistorical Monarchs|ja|欠史八代}} * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka Period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} [[Category:Wani Clan]] [[Category:Japanese Empresses]] [[Category:Empress Dowagers of Japan]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Figures]] [[Category:Female Imperial Family Members Before the Kofun Period]] [[Category:Birth and Death Dates Unknown]] [[ja:押媛]] {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Oshihime}} [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[en:Oshihime]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Oshihime]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Oshihime]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Oshihime]][[en:Oshihime]][[ja:押媛]]

Daughter of {{ill|Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko|ja|十市県主五十坂彦}}, named Isosaka-hime }} '''Oshihime''' (押媛) is a figure from ancient Japan. == Overview == Alternate titles include '''Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto''' (忍鹿比売命) and Oshihime no Mikoto (押媛命). She is featured in historical texts such as the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, and {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}. Oshihime is described as the consort of {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}}, mother to {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}} and {{ill|Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|ja|大吉備諸進命}}. She is said to be the daughter of {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命}}, making her both the niece and consort of Emperor Kōan. However, in the first supplementary text of the Nihon Shoki regarding Emperor Kōan, the empress is identified as Nagahime, daughter of Shiki Agatanushi Hae. In the second supplementary text, the empress is named Isosaka-hime, daughter of Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko. [[File:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|thumb|right|400px|Family tree of emperors (1st–10th) [[Media:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|View SVG (browser support required)]]]] == References == The {{ill|Fuji Clan|ja|富士氏}}, who served as grand priests of {{ill|Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha|ja|富士山本宮浅間大社}}, trace their lineage to the Wani clan and preserved the Wani genealogy (see {{ill|Wani Clan|ja|和珥氏}}). According to this genealogy, Oshihime is listed as the daughter of {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命}}, with an alternate name of Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto. She is recorded as Emperor Kōan's empress, while her brother is identified as Wani Hikoshihito no Mikoto. The accuracy of this record remains disputed. {{ill|Higo Ryūkai|ja|比護隆界}} claimed that these genealogies were fabricated in later periods.Higo Ryūkai, "Formation and Reliability of Clan Genealogies: On the 'Wani Clan Genealogy' from Suruga Sengen Shrine," *Japanese Ancient History Essays*, 1988. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == Sources == * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō (historian)|ja|坂本太郎}} and Hirano Kunio, *Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Comprehensive Overview of the Imperial Family Lineage* (Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha) * {{ill|Ōta Ryō|ja|太田亮}}, *Dictionary of Surnames and Genealogies* (Kadokawa Shoten) == Related Topics == * {{ill|Eight Unhistorical Monarchs|ja|欠史八代}} * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka Period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} (historian)|ja|坂本太郎}} and Hirano Kunio, *Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Comprehensive Overview of the Imperial Family Lineage* (Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha) * {{ill|Ōta revision.text:
{{Infobox Empress | name = Oshihime (押媛) | image = | caption = | reign = | era_name_reign = | imperial_consort = Emperor Kōan (孝安天皇) February 14, 26th year of Emperor Kōan | empress_dowager = Emperor Kōrei (孝霊天皇) January 12, 1st year of Emperor Kōrei | birth_date = | birth_place = | death_date = | death_place = | burial_place = | clan = {{ill|Wani Clan|ja|和邇氏}} | father = {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命}} | spouse1 = {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}} | children = {{ill|Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|ja|大吉備諸進命}}
{{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}} | other_titles = Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto (忍鹿比売命)
Oshihime no Mikoto (押媛命) | other_theories = Daughter of {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi Hae|ja|磯城県主葉江}}, named Nagahime
Daughter of {{ill|Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko|ja|十市県主五十坂彦}}, named Isosaka-hime }} '''Oshihime''' (押媛) is a figure from ancient Japan. == Overview == Alternate titles include '''Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto''' (忍鹿比売命) and Oshihime no Mikoto (押媛命). She is featured in historical texts such as the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, and {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}. Oshihime is described as the consort of {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}}, mother to {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}} and {{ill|Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|ja|大吉備諸進命}}. She is said to be the daughter of {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命}}, making her both the niece and consort of Emperor Kōan. However, in the first supplementary text of the Nihon Shoki regarding Emperor Kōan, the empress is identified as Nagahime, daughter of Shiki Agatanushi Hae. In the second supplementary text, the empress is named Isosaka-hime, daughter of Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko. [[File:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|thumb|right|400px|Family tree of emperors (1st–10th) [[Media:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|View SVG (browser support required)]]]] == References == The {{ill|Fuji Clan|ja|富士氏}}, who served as grand priests of {{ill|Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha|ja|富士山本宮浅間大社}}, trace their lineage to the Wani clan and preserved the Wani genealogy (see {{ill|Wani Clan|ja|和珥氏}}). According to this genealogy, Oshihime is listed as the daughter of {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命}}, with an alternate name of Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto. She is recorded as Emperor Kōan's empress, while her brother is identified as Wani Hikoshihito no Mikoto. The accuracy of this record remains disputed. {{ill|Higo Ryūkai|ja|比護隆界}} claimed that these genealogies were fabricated in later periods.Higo Ryūkai, "Formation and Reliability of Clan Genealogies: On the 'Wani Clan Genealogy' from Suruga Sengen Shrine," *Japanese Ancient History Essays*, 1988. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == Sources == * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō (historian)|ja|坂本太郎}} and Hirano Kunio, *Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Comprehensive Overview of the Imperial Family Lineage* (Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha) * {{ill|Ōta Ryō|ja|太田亮}}, *Dictionary of Surnames and Genealogies* (Kadokawa Shoten) == Related Topics == * {{ill|Eight Unhistorical Monarchs|ja|欠史八代}} * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka Period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} revision.comment: Text replacement - "== Related Topics ==" to "== See Also =="
revision.text:
{{Infobox royalty | name = Oshihime (押媛) | image = | caption = | reign = | era_name_reign = | imperial_consort = Emperor Kōan (孝安天皇) February 14, 26th year of Emperor Kōan | empress_dowager = Emperor Kōrei (孝霊天皇) January 12, 1st year of Emperor Kōrei | birth_date = | birth_place = | death_date = | death_place = | burial_place = | clan = {{ill|Wani Clan|ja|和邇氏}} | father = {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命}} | spouse1 = {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}} | children = {{ill|Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|ja|大吉備諸進命}}
{{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}} | other_titles = Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto (忍鹿比売命)
Oshihime no Mikoto (押媛命) | other_theories = Daughter of {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi Hae|ja|磯城県主葉江}}, named Nagahime
Daughter of {{ill|Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko|ja|十市県主五十坂彦}}, named Isosaka-hime }} '''Oshihime''' (押媛) is a figure from ancient Japan. == Overview == Alternate titles include '''Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto''' (忍鹿比売命) and Oshihime no Mikoto (押媛命). She is featured in historical texts such as the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, and {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}. Oshihime is described as the consort of {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}}, mother to {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}} and {{ill|Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|ja|大吉備諸進命}}. She is said to be the daughter of {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命}}, making her both the niece and consort of Emperor Kōan. However, in the first supplementary text of the Nihon Shoki regarding Emperor Kōan, the empress is identified as Nagahime, daughter of Shiki Agatanushi Hae. In the second supplementary text, the empress is named Isosaka-hime, daughter of Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko. [[File:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|thumb|right|400px|Family tree of emperors (1st–10th) [[Media:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|View SVG (browser support required)]]]] == References == The {{ill|Fuji Clan|ja|富士氏}}, who served as grand priests of {{ill|Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha|ja|富士山本宮浅間大社}}, trace their lineage to the Wani clan and preserved the Wani genealogy (see {{ill|Wani Clan|ja|和珥氏}}). According to this genealogy, Oshihime is listed as the daughter of {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命}}, with an alternate name of Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto. She is recorded as Emperor Kōan's empress, while her brother is identified as Wani Hikoshihito no Mikoto. The accuracy of this record remains disputed. {{ill|Higo Ryūkai|ja|比護隆界}} claimed that these genealogies were fabricated in later periods.Higo Ryūkai, "Formation and Reliability of Clan Genealogies: On the 'Wani Clan Genealogy' from Suruga Sengen Shrine," *Japanese Ancient History Essays*, 1988. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == Sources == * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō (historian)|ja|坂本太郎}} and Hirano Kunio, *Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Comprehensive Overview of the Imperial Family Lineage* (Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha) * {{ill|Ōta Ryō|ja|太田亮}}, *Dictionary of Surnames and Genealogies* (Kadokawa Shoten) == See Also == * {{ill|Eight Unhistorical Monarchs|ja|欠史八代}} * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka Period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} revision.text:
{{Infobox royalty | name = Oshihime (押媛) | image = | caption = | reign = | era_name_reign = | imperial_consort = Emperor Kōan (孝安天皇) February 14, 26th year of Emperor Kōan | empress_dowager = Emperor Kōrei (孝霊天皇) January 12, 1st year of Emperor Kōrei | birth_date = | birth_place = | death_date = | death_place = | burial_place = | clan = {{ill|Wani Clan|ja|和邇氏}} | father = {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命}} | spouse1 = {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}} | children = {{ill|Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|ja|大吉備諸進命}}
{{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}} | other_titles = Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto (忍鹿比売命)
Oshihime no Mikoto (押媛命) | other_theories = Daughter of {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi Hae|ja|磯城県主葉江}}, named Nagahime
Daughter of {{ill|Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko|ja|十市県主五十坂彦}}, named Isosaka-hime }} '''Oshihime''' (押媛) is a figure from ancient Japan. == Overview == Alternate titles include '''Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto''' (忍鹿比売命) and Oshihime no Mikoto (押媛命). She is featured in historical texts such as the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, and {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}. Oshihime is described as the consort of {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}}, mother to {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}} and {{ill|Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|ja|大吉備諸進命}}. She is said to be the daughter of {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命}}, making her both the niece and consort of Emperor Kōan. However, in the first supplementary text of the Nihon Shoki regarding Emperor Kōan, the empress is identified as Nagahime, daughter of Shiki Agatanushi Hae. In the second supplementary text, the empress is named Isosaka-hime, daughter of Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko. [[File:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|thumb|right|400px|Family tree of emperors (1st–10th) [[Media:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|View SVG (browser support required)]]]] == References == The {{ill|Fuji Clan|ja|富士氏}}, who served as grand priests of {{ill|Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha|ja|富士山本宮浅間大社}}, trace their lineage to the Wani clan and preserved the Wani genealogy (see {{ill|Wani Clan|ja|和珥氏}}). According to this genealogy, Oshihime is listed as the daughter of {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命}}, with an alternate name of Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto. She is recorded as Emperor Kōan's empress, while her brother is identified as Wani Hikoshihito no Mikoto. The accuracy of this record remains disputed. {{ill|Higo Ryūkai|ja|比護隆界}} claimed that these genealogies were fabricated in later periods.Higo Ryūkai, "Formation and Reliability of Clan Genealogies: On the 'Wani Clan Genealogy' from Suruga Sengen Shrine," *Japanese Ancient History Essays*, 1988. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == Sources == * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō (historian)|ja|坂本太郎}} and Hirano Kunio, *Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Comprehensive Overview of the Imperial Family Lineage* (Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha) * {{ill|Ōta Ryō|ja|太田亮}}, *Dictionary of Surnames and Genealogies* (Kadokawa Shoten) == See Also == * {{ill|Eight Unhistorical Monarchs|ja|欠史八代}} * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka Period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} In the second supplementary text, the empress is named Isosaka-hime, daughter of Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko. [[File:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|thumb|right|400px|Family tree of emperors (1st–10th) [[Media:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|View SVG (browser support required)]]]] == References == The {{ill|Fuji Clan|ja|富士氏}}, who served as grand priests of {{ill|Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha|ja|富士山本宮浅間大社}}, trace their lineage to the Wani clan and preserved the Wani genealogy (see {{ill|Wani Clan|ja|和珥氏}}). According to this genealogy, Oshihime is listed as the daughter of {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命}}, with an alternate name of Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto. She is recorded as Emperor Kōan's empress, while her brother is identified as Wani Hikoshihito no Mikoto. The accuracy of this record remains disputed. {{ill|Higo Ryūkai|ja|比護隆界}} claimed that these genealogies were fabricated in later periods.Higo Ryūkai, "Formation and Reliability of Clan Genealogies: On the 'Wani Clan Genealogy' from Suruga Sengen Shrine," *Japanese Ancient History Essays*, 1988. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == Sources == * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō (historian)|ja|坂本太郎}} and Hirano Kunio, *Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Comprehensive Overview of the Imperial Family Lineage* (Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha) * {{ill|Ōta Ryō|ja|太田亮}}, *Dictionary of Surnames and Genealogies* (Kadokawa Shoten) == See Also == * {{ill|Eight Unhistorical Monarchs|ja|欠史八代}} * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka Period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} Oshihime no Mikoto (押媛命) | other_theories = Daughter of {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi Hae|ja|磯城県主葉江}}, named Nagahime
Daughter of {{ill|Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko|ja|十市県主五十坂彦}}, named Isosaka-hime }} '''Oshihime''' (押媛) is a figure from ancient Japan. == Overview == Alternate titles include '''Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto''' (忍鹿比売命) and Oshihime no Mikoto (押媛命). She is featured in historical texts such as the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, and {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}. Oshihime is described as the consort of {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}}, mother to {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}} and {{ill|Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|ja|大吉備諸進命}}. She is said to be the daughter of {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命|1=Prince Ameoshitarashi}}, making her both the niece and consort of Emperor Kōan. However, in the first supplementary text of the Nihon Shoki regarding Emperor Kōan, the empress is identified as Nagahime, daughter of Shiki Agatanushi Hae. In the second supplementary text, the empress is named Isosaka-hime, daughter of Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko. [[File:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|thumb|right|400px|Family tree of emperors (1st–10th) [[Media:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|View SVG (browser support required)]]]] == References == The {{ill|Fuji Clan|ja|富士氏}}, who served as grand priests of {{ill|Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha|ja|富士山本宮浅間大社}}, trace their lineage to the Wani clan and preserved the Wani genealogy (see {{ill|Wani Clan|ja|和珥氏}}). According to this genealogy, Oshihime is listed as the daughter of {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命|1=Prince Ameoshitarashi}}, with an alternate name of Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto. She is recorded as Emperor Kōan's empress, while her brother is identified as Wani Hikoshihito no Mikoto. The accuracy of this record remains disputed. {{ill|Higo Ryūkai|ja|比護隆界}} claimed that these genealogies were fabricated in later periods.Higo Ryūkai, "Formation and Reliability of Clan Genealogies: On the 'Wani Clan Genealogy' from Suruga Sengen Shrine," *Japanese Ancient History Essays*, 1988. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == Sources == * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō (historian)|ja|坂本太郎}} and Hirano Kunio, *Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Comprehensive Overview of the Imperial Family Lineage* (Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha) * {{ill|Ōta Ryō|ja|太田亮}}, *Dictionary of Surnames and Genealogies* (Kadokawa Shoten) == See Also == * {{ill|Eight Unhistorical Monarchs|ja|欠史八代}} * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka Period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} revision.comment: [[Special:Contributions/学者気取りの先頭はここで終わり！|学者気取りの先頭はここで終わり！]] ([[User talk:学者気取りの先頭はここで終わり！|会話]]) による ID:100123606 の版を[[H:RV|取り消し]]
revision.text:
{{基礎情報 皇后 |名 = 押媛 |立后根拠 = 第6代天皇后 |画像 = |画像サイズ = |画像説明 = |画像代替文 = |在位期間 = |和暦在位期間 = |皇后 = 孝安天皇26年2月14日 |皇太后 = 孝霊天皇元年1月12日 |出生日 = |生地 = |死亡日 = |没地 = |陵所 = |諱 = |諡号 = |異称 = |幼称 = |氏族 = [[和邇氏]] |父親 = [[天足彦国押人命]] |母親 = |配偶者1 = [[孝安天皇]] |結婚 = |子女 =[[大吉備諸進命]]
[[孝霊天皇]] |養子女 = |皇居 = |空欄表題1 = 異表記 |空欄記載1 = 忍鹿比売命（古事記）
押媛命（先代旧事本紀） |空欄表題2 = 異説 |空欄記載2 = 磯城県主[[葉江]]の娘・長媛
十市県主五十坂彦の娘・五十坂媛 |脚注 = }} '''押媛'''（おしひめ）は古代日本の人物。 == 概要 == 異表記として'''忍鹿比売命'''（おしかひめのみこと）、押媛命（おしひめのみこと）などが存在する。 『[[古事記]]』、『[[日本書紀]]』、『[[先代旧事本紀]]』など各史料に登場する女性で、[[孝安天皇]]の[[皇后]]として[[孝霊天皇]]と[[大吉備諸進命]]を生む。[[天足彦国押人命|天足彦国押人]]の娘かつ孝安天皇の姪とされる。 ただし『日本書紀』第1の一書での孝安天皇の皇后は、磯城県主[[葉江]]の娘である[[長媛]]で、第2の一書では十市県主五十坂彦の娘である五十坂媛とされている。 [[ファイル:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|thumb|right|400px|天皇略系図（初代 - 第10代） [[メディア:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|SVGで表示（対応ブラウザのみ）]]]] == 参考 == [[富士山本宮浅間大社|駿河浅間大社]]の大宮司家（[[富士氏]]）は和爾部姓で和邇系図（[[和珥氏]]を参照）を伝えており、真偽は詳らかでないが、[[孝昭天皇]]の皇子[[天足彦国押人命]]の子として「押媛命（一に忍鹿比売命、母は建田背命の妹 宇那比媛命也。孝安天皇の皇后）弟 和爾日子押人命（一に稚押彦命、母同上。大倭和邇里に居る）」と記載されている[[太田亮]]『姓氏家系大辞典』[[角川書店]]（1963年）第三巻P6661より。。 == 脚注 == {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == *[[坂本太郎 (歴史学者)|坂本太郎]]・[[平野邦雄]]『日本古代人名辭典』（[[吉川弘文館]]） *『天皇家系譜総覧』（[[新人物往来社]]） *[[太田亮]]『姓氏家系大辞典』（[[角川書店]]） == 関連項目 == *[[欠史八代]] *[[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]] {{歴代皇后一覧}} {{歴代日本の皇太后一覧}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おしひめ}} [[Category:和珥氏]] [[Category:日本の皇后]] [[Category:日本の皇太后]] [[Category:古代日本の人物]] [[Category:古墳時代以前の女性皇族]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

{{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}} | other_titles = Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto (忍鹿比売命)
Oshihime no Mikoto (押媛命) | other_theories = Daughter of {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi Hae|ja|磯城県主葉江}}, named Nagahime
Daughter of {{ill|Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko|ja|十市県主五十坂彦}}, named Isosaka-hime }} '''Oshihime''' (押媛) is a figure from ancient Japan. == Overview == Alternate titles include '''Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto''' (忍鹿比売命) and Oshihime no Mikoto (押媛命). She is featured in historical texts such as the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, and {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}. Oshihime is described as the consort of {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}}, mother to {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}} and {{ill|Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|ja|大吉備諸進命}}. She is said to be the daughter of {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命|1=Prince Ameoshitarashi}}, making her both the niece and consort of Emperor Kōan. However, in the first supplementary text of the Nihon Shoki regarding Emperor Kōan, the empress is identified as Nagahime, daughter of Shiki Agatanushi Hae. In the second supplementary text, the empress is named Isosaka-hime, daughter of Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko. [[File:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|thumb|right|400px|Family tree of emperors (1st–10th) [[Media:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|View SVG (browser support required)]]]] == References == The {{ill|Fuji Clan|ja|富士氏}}, who served as grand priests of {{ill|Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha|ja|富士山本宮浅間大社}}, trace their lineage to the Wani clan and preserved the Wani genealogy (see {{ill|Wani Clan|ja|和珥氏}}). According to this genealogy, Oshihime is listed as the daughter of {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命|1=Prince Ameoshitarashi}}, with an alternate name of Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto. She is recorded as Emperor Kōan's empress, while her brother is identified as Wani Hikoshihito no Mikoto. The accuracy of this record remains disputed. {{ill|Higo Ryūkai|ja|比護隆界}} claimed that these genealogies were fabricated in later periods.Higo Ryūkai, "Formation and Reliability of Clan Genealogies: On the 'Wani Clan Genealogy' from Suruga Sengen Shrine," *Japanese Ancient History Essays*, 1988. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == Sources == * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō (historian)|ja|坂本太郎}} and Hirano Kunio, *Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Comprehensive Overview of the Imperial Family Lineage* (Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha) * {{ill|Ōta Ryō|ja|太田亮}}, *Dictionary of Surnames and Genealogies* (Kadokawa Shoten) == See Also == * {{ill|Eight Unhistorical Monarchs|ja|欠史八代}} * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka Period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} [[Category:Wani Clan]] [[Category:Japanese Empresses]] [[Category:Empress Dowagers of Japan]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Figures]] [[Category:Female Imperial Family Members Before the Kofun Period]] [[Category:Birth and Death Dates Unknown]] [[ja:押媛]] {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Oshihime}} [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[en:Oshihime]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Oshihime]][[Category:Oshihime]][[Category:Oshihime]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Oshihime]][[Category:Oshihime]][[Category:Oshihime]][[Category:Oshihime]][[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[sn:Oshihime]][[Category:11]][[sn:Oshihime]][[Category:11]] [[fa:%D8%A7%D9%88%D8%B4%DB%8C %D9%87%DB%8C%D9%85%D9%87]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Oshihime]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Oshihime]][[en:Oshihime]][[ja:押媛]]

The accuracy of this record remains disputed. {{ill|Higo Ryūkai|ja|比護隆界|12=draft}} claimed that these genealogies were fabricated in later periods.Higo Ryūkai, "Formation and Reliability of Clan Genealogies: On the 'Wani Clan Genealogy' from Suruga Sengen Shrine," *Japanese Ancient History Essays*, 1988. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == Sources == * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō (historian)|ja|坂本太郎|12=draft}} and Hirano Kunio, *Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Comprehensive Overview of the Imperial Family Lineage* (Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha) * {{ill|Ōta Ryō|ja|太田亮|12=draft}}, *Dictionary of Surnames and Genealogies* (Kadokawa Shoten)== See Also == * {{ill|Eight Unhistorical Monarchs|ja|欠史八代|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka Period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=draft}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Oshihime]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Oshihime]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Oshihime]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Wani Clan]] [[Category:Japanese Empresses]] [[Category:Empress Dowagers of Japan]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Figures]] [[Category:Female Imperial Family Members Before the Kofun Period]] [[Category:Birth and Death Dates Unknown]] [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Oshihime]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:11]] }} [[ja:押媛]] [[en:Oshihime]] [[sn:Oshihime]] [[fa:%D8%A7%D9%88%D8%B4%DB%8C %D9%87%DB%8C%D9%85%D9%87]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Oshihime}}

{{Infobox royalty | name = Oshihime (押媛) | image = | caption = | reign = | era_name_reign = | imperial_consort = Emperor Kōan (孝安天皇) February 14, 26th year of Emperor Kōan | empress_dowager = Emperor Kōrei (孝霊天皇) January 12, 1st year of Emperor Kōrei | birth_date = | birth_place = | death_date = | death_place = | burial_place = | clan = {{ill|Wani Clan|ja|和邇氏|12=draft}} | father = {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命|1=Prince Ameoshitarashi|12=draft}} | spouse1 = {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇|12=draft}} | children = {{ill|Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|ja|大吉備諸進命|12=draft}}
{{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇|12=draft}} | other_titles = Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto (忍鹿比売命)
Oshihime no Mikoto (押媛命) | other_theories = Daughter of {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi Hae|ja|磯城県主葉江|12=draft}}, named Nagahime
Daughter of {{ill|Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko|ja|十市県主五十坂彦|12=draft}}, named Isosaka-hime }} '''Oshihime''' (押媛) is a figure from ancient Japan. == Overview == Alternate titles include '''Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto''' (忍鹿比売命) and Oshihime no Mikoto (押媛命). She is featured in historical texts such as the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft}}. Oshihime is described as the consort of {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇|12=draft}}, mother to {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|ja|大吉備諸進命|12=draft}}. She is said to be the daughter of {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命|1=Prince Ameoshitarashi|12=draft}}, making her both the niece and consort of Emperor Kōan. However, in the first supplementary text of the Nihon Shoki regarding Emperor Kōan, the empress is identified as Nagahime, daughter of Shiki Agatanushi Hae. In the second supplementary text, the empress is named Isosaka-hime, daughter of Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko. [[File:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|thumb|right|400px|Family tree of emperors (1st–10th) [[Media:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|View SVG (browser support required)]]]] == References == The {{ill|Fuji Clan|ja|富士氏|12=draft}}, who served as grand priests of {{ill|Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha|ja|富士山本宮浅間大社|12=draft}}, trace their lineage to the Wani clan and preserved the Wani genealogy (see {{ill|Wani Clan|ja|和珥氏|12=draft}}). According to this genealogy, Oshihime is listed as the daughter of {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命|1=Prince Ameoshitarashi|12=draft}}, with an alternate name of Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto. She is recorded as Emperor Kōan's empress, while her brother is identified as Wani Hikoshihito no Mikoto. The accuracy of this record remains disputed. {{ill|Higo Ryūkai|ja|比護隆界|12=draft}} claimed that these genealogies were fabricated in later periods.Higo Ryūkai, "Formation and Reliability of Clan Genealogies: On the 'Wani Clan Genealogy' from Suruga Sengen Shrine," *Japanese Ancient History Essays*, 1988. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == Sources == * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō (historian)|ja|坂本太郎|12=draft}} and Hirano Kunio, *Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Comprehensive Overview of the Imperial Family Lineage* (Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha) * {{ill|Ōta Ryō|ja|太田亮|12=draft}}, *Dictionary of Surnames and Genealogies* (Kadokawa Shoten)== See Also == * {{ill|Eight Unhistorical Monarchs|ja|欠史八代|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka Period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=draft}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Oshihime]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Oshihime]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Oshihime]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Wani Clan]] [[Category:Japanese Empresses]] [[Category:Empress Dowagers of Japan]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Figures]] [[Category:Female Imperial Family Members Before the Kofun Period]] [[Category:Birth and Death Dates Unknown]] [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Oshihime]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:11]] }} [[ja:押媛]] [[en:Oshihime]] [[sn:Oshihime]] [[fa:%D8%A7%D9%88%D8%B4%DB%8C %D9%87%DB%8C%D9%85%D9%87]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Oshihime}}

Oshihime is described as the consort of {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇|12=draft}}, mother to {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|ja|大吉備諸進命|12=draft}}. She is said to be the daughter of {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命|1=Prince Ameoshitarashi|12=draft}}, making her both the niece and consort of Emperor Kōan. However, in the first supplementary text of the Nihon Shoki regarding Emperor Kōan, the empress is identified as Nagahime, daughter of Shiki Agatanushi Hae. In the second supplementary text, the empress is named Isosaka-hime, daughter of Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko. [[File:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|thumb|right|400px|Family tree of emperors (1st–10th) [[Media:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|View SVG (browser support required)]]]] == References == The {{ill|Fuji clan|ja|富士氏|12=draft}}, who served as grand priests of {{ill|Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha|ja|富士山本宮浅間大社|12=draft}}, trace their lineage to the Wani clan and preserved the Wani genealogy (see {{ill|Wani Clan|ja|和珥氏|12=draft}}). According to this genealogy, Oshihime is listed as the daughter of {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命|1=Prince Ameoshitarashi|12=draft}}, with an alternate name of Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto. She is recorded as Emperor Kōan's empress, while her brother is identified as Wani Hikoshihito no Mikoto. The accuracy of this record remains disputed. {{ill|Higo Ryūkai|ja|比護隆界|12=draft}} claimed that these genealogies were fabricated in later periods.Higo Ryūkai, "Formation and Reliability of Clan Genealogies: On the 'Wani Clan Genealogy' from Suruga Sengen Shrine," *Japanese Ancient History Essays*, 1988. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == Sources == * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō (historian)|ja|坂本太郎|12=draft}} and Hirano Kunio, *Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Comprehensive Overview of the Imperial Family Lineage* (Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha) * {{ill|Ōta Ryō|ja|太田亮|12=draft}}, *Dictionary of Surnames and Genealogies* (Kadokawa Shoten)== See Also == * {{ill|Eight Unhistorical Monarchs|ja|欠史八代|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka Period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=draft}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Oshihime]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Oshihime]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Oshihime]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Wani Clan]] [[Category:Japanese Empresses]] [[Category:Empress Dowagers of Japan]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Figures]] [[Category:Female Imperial Family Members Before the Kofun Period]] [[Category:Birth and Death Dates Unknown]] [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Oshihime]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Oshihime]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:押媛]] [[en:Oshihime]] [[sn:Oshihime]] [[fa:%D8%A7%D9%88%D8%B4%DB%8C %D9%87%DB%8C%D9%85%D9%87]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Oshihime}} [[Category:New]]

The accuracy of this record remains disputed. {{ill|Higo Ryūkai|ja|比護隆界|12=draft|12=draft}} claimed that these genealogies were fabricated in later periods.Higo Ryūkai, "Formation and Reliability of Clan Genealogies: On the 'Wani Clan Genealogy' from Suruga Sengen Shrine," *Japanese Ancient History Essays*, 1988. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == Sources == * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō (historian)|ja|坂本太郎|12=draft|12=draft}} and Hirano Kunio, *Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Comprehensive Overview of the Imperial Family Lineage* (Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha) * {{ill|Ōta Ryō|ja|太田亮|12=draft|12=draft}}, *Dictionary of Surnames and Genealogies* (Kadokawa Shoten)== See Also == * {{ill|Eight Unhistorical Monarchs|ja|欠史八代|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka Period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Oshihime]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Oshihime]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Oshihime]] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Oshihime]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Oshihime]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wani Clan]] [[Category:Japanese Empresses]] [[Category:Empress Dowagers of Japan]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Figures]] [[Category:Female Imperial Family Members Before the Kofun Period]] [[Category:Birth and Death Dates Unknown]] [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Oshihime]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Oshihime]] [[ja:押媛]] [[en:Oshihime]] [[fa:%D8%A7%D9%88%D8%B4%DB%8C %D9%87%DB%8C%D9%85%D9%87]] {{citation needed|Oshihime}}

{{Infobox royalty | name = Oshihime (押媛) | image = | caption = | reign = | era_name_reign = | imperial_consort = Emperor Kōan (孝安天皇) February 14, 26th year of Emperor Kōan | empress_dowager = Emperor Kōrei (孝霊天皇) January 12, 1st year of Emperor Kōrei | birth_date = | birth_place = | death_date = | death_place = | burial_place = | clan = {{ill|Wani Clan|ja|和邇氏}} | father = {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命|1=Prince Ameoshitarashi}} | spouse1 = {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}} | children = {{ill|Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|ja|大吉備諸進命}}
{{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}} | other_titles = Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto (忍鹿比売命)
Oshihime no Mikoto (押媛命) | other_theories = Daughter of {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi Hae|ja|磯城県主葉江}}, named Nagahime
Daughter of {{ill|Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko|ja|十市県主五十坂彦}}, named Isosaka-hime }} '''Oshihime''' (押媛) is a figure from ancient Japan. == Overview == Alternate titles include '''Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto''' (忍鹿比売命) and Oshihime no Mikoto (押媛命). She is featured in historical texts such as the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, and {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}. Oshihime is described as the consort of {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}}, mother to {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}} and {{ill|Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|ja|大吉備諸進命}}. She is said to be the daughter of {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命|1=Prince Ameoshitarashi}}, making her both the niece and consort of Emperor Kōan. However, in the first supplementary text of the Nihon Shoki regarding Emperor Kōan, the empress is identified as Nagahime, daughter of Shiki Agatanushi Hae. In the second supplementary text, the empress is named Isosaka-hime, daughter of Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko. [[File:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|thumb|right|400px|Family tree of emperors (1st–10th) [[Media:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|View SVG (browser support required)]]]] == References == The {{ill|Fuji clan|ja|富士氏}}, who served as grand priests of {{ill|Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha|ja|富士山本宮浅間大社}}, trace their lineage to the Wani clan and preserved the Wani genealogy (see {{ill|Wani Clan|ja|和珥氏}}). According to this genealogy, Oshihime is listed as the daughter of {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命|1=Prince Ameoshitarashi}}, with an alternate name of Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto. She is recorded as Emperor Kōan's empress, while her brother is identified as Wani Hikoshihito no Mikoto. The accuracy of this record remains disputed. {{ill|Higo Ryūkai|ja|比護隆界}} claimed that these genealogies were fabricated in later periods.Higo Ryūkai, "Formation and Reliability of Clan Genealogies: On the 'Wani Clan Genealogy' from Suruga Sengen Shrine," *Japanese Ancient History Essays*, 1988. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == Sources == * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō (historian)|ja|坂本太郎}} and Hirano Kunio, *Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Comprehensive Overview of the Imperial Family Lineage* (Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha) * {{ill|Ōta Ryō|ja|太田亮}}, *Dictionary of Surnames and Genealogies* (Kadokawa Shoten)== See Also == * {{ill|Eight Unhistorical Monarchs|ja|欠史八代}} * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka Period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Oshihime]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Oshihime]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Oshihime]] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Oshihime]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Oshihime]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wani Clan]] [[Category:Japanese Empresses]] [[Category:Empress Dowagers of Japan]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Figures]] [[Category:Female Imperial Family Members Before the Kofun Period]] [[Category:Birth and Death Dates Unknown]] [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Oshihime]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Oshihime]] [[ja:押媛]] [[en:Oshihime]] [[fa:%D8%A7%D9%88%D8%B4%DB%8C %D9%87%DB%8C%D9%85%D9%87]] {{citation needed|Oshihime}}

revision.text:
{{基礎情報 皇后 |名 = 押媛 |立后根拠 = 第6代天皇后 |画像 = |画像サイズ = |画像説明 = |画像代替文 = |在位期間 = |和暦在位期間 = |皇后 = 孝安天皇26年2月14日 |皇太后 = 孝霊天皇元年1月12日 |出生日 = |生地 = |死亡日 = |没地 = |陵所 = |諱 = |諡号 = |異称 = |幼称 = |氏族 = [[和邇氏]] |父親 = [[天足彦国押人命]] |母親 = |配偶者1 = [[孝安天皇]] |結婚 = |子女 =[[大吉備諸進命]]
[[孝霊天皇]] |養子女 = |皇居 = |空欄表題1 = 異表記 |空欄記載1 = 忍鹿比売命（古事記）
押媛命（先代旧事本紀） |空欄表題2 = 異説 |空欄記載2 = 磯城県主[[葉江]]の娘・長媛
十市県主五十坂彦の娘・五十坂媛 |脚注 = }} '''押媛'''（おしひめ）は古代日本の人物。 == 概要 == 異表記として'''忍鹿比売命'''（おしかひめのみこと）、押媛命（おしひめのみこと）などが存在する。 『[[古事記]]』、『[[日本書紀]]』、『[[先代旧事本紀]]』など各史料に登場する女性で、[[孝安天皇]]の[[皇后]]として[[孝霊天皇]]と[[大吉備諸進命]]を生む。[[天足彦国押人命|天足彦国押人]]の娘かつ孝安天皇の姪とされる。 ただし『日本書紀』第1の一書での孝安天皇の皇后は、磯城県主[[葉江]]の娘である[[長媛]]で、第2の一書では十市県主五十坂彦の娘である五十坂媛とされている。 [[ファイル:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|thumb|right|400px|天皇略系図（初代 - 第10代） [[メディア:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|SVGで表示（対応ブラウザのみ）]]]] == 参考 == [[富士山本宮浅間大社|駿河浅間大社]]の大宮司家（[[富士氏]]）は和爾部姓で和邇系図（[[和珥氏]]を参照）を伝えており、真偽は詳らかでないが、[[孝昭天皇]]の皇子[[天足彦国押人命]]の子として「押媛命（一に忍鹿比売命、母は建田背命の妹 宇那比媛命也。孝安天皇の皇后）弟 和爾日子押人命（一に稚押彦命、母同上。大倭和邇里に居る）」と記載されている[[太田亮]]『姓氏家系大辞典』[[角川書店]]（1963年）第三巻P6661より。。 == 脚注 == {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == *[[坂本太郎 (歴史学者)|坂本太郎]]・[[平野邦雄]]『日本古代人名辭典』（[[吉川弘文館]]） *『天皇家系譜総覧』（[[新人物往来社]]） *[[太田亮]]『姓氏家系大辞典』（[[角川書店]]） == 関連項目 == *[[欠史八代]] *[[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]] {{歴代皇后一覧}} {{歴代日本の皇太后一覧}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おしひめ}} [[Category:和珥氏]] [[Category:日本の皇后]] [[Category:日本の皇太后]] [[Category:古代日本の人物]] [[Category:古墳時代以前の女性皇族]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

The accuracy of this record remains disputed. {{ill|Higo Ryūkai|ja|比護隆界|12=draft}} claimed that these genealogies were fabricated in later periods.Higo Ryūkai, "Formation and Reliability of Clan Genealogies: On the 'Wani Clan Genealogy' from Suruga Sengen Shrine," *Japanese Ancient History Essays*, 1988. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == Sources == * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō (historian)|ja|坂本太郎|12=draft}} and Hirano Kunio, *Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Comprehensive Overview of the Imperial Family Lineage* (Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha) * {{ill|Ōta Ryō|ja|太田亮|12=draft}}, *Dictionary of Surnames and Genealogies* (Kadokawa Shoten)== See Also == * {{ill|Eight Unhistorical Monarchs|ja|欠史八代|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka Period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=draft}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Oshihime]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Oshihime]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Oshihime]] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Oshihime}} {{translated page|ja|押媛|version=104216939|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:Wani Clan]] [[Category:Japanese Empresses]] [[Category:Empress Dowagers of Japan]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Figures]] [[Category:Female Imperial Family Members Before the Kofun Period]] [[Category:Birth and Death Dates Unknown]] [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Oshihime]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] }} [[sn:Oshihime]] [[ja:押媛]] [[en:Oshihime]] [[fa:%D8%A7%D9%88%D8%B4%DB%8C %D9%87%DB%8C%D9%85%D9%87]]
