{{Infobox royalty | name = Oshihime (押媛) | image = | caption = | reign = | era_name_reign = | imperial_consort = Emperor Kōan (孝安天皇) February 14, 26th year of Emperor Kōan | empress_dowager = Emperor Kōrei (孝霊天皇) January 12, 1st year of Emperor Kōrei | birth_date = | birth_place = | death_date = | death_place = | burial_place = | clan = {{ill|Wani Clan|ja|和邇氏|12=draft}} | father = {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命|1=Prince Ameoshitarashi|12=draft}} | spouse1 = {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇|12=draft}} | children = {{ill|Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|ja|大吉備諸進命|12=draft}}
{{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇|12=draft}} | other_titles = Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto (忍鹿比売命)
Oshihime no Mikoto (押媛命) | other_theories = Daughter of {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi Hae|ja|磯城県主葉江|12=draft}}, named Nagahime
Daughter of {{ill|Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko|ja|十市県主五十坂彦|12=draft}}, named Isosaka-hime }} '''Oshihime''' (押媛) is a figure from ancient Japan. == Overview == Alternate titles include '''Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto''' (忍鹿比売命) and Oshihime no Mikoto (押媛命). She is featured in historical texts such as the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft}}. Oshihime is described as the consort of {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇|12=draft}}, mother to {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|ja|大吉備諸進命|12=draft}}. She is said to be the daughter of {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命|1=Prince Ameoshitarashi|12=draft}}, making her both the niece and consort of Emperor Kōan. However, in the first supplementary text of the Nihon Shoki regarding Emperor Kōan, the empress is identified as Nagahime, daughter of Shiki Agatanushi Hae. In the second supplementary text, the empress is named Isosaka-hime, daughter of Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko. [[File:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|thumb|right|400px|Family tree of emperors (1st–10th) [[Media:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|View SVG (browser support required)]]]] == References == The {{ill|Fuji clan|ja|富士氏|12=draft}}, who served as grand priests of {{ill|Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha|ja|富士山本宮浅間大社|12=draft}}, trace their lineage to the Wani clan and preserved the Wani genealogy (see {{ill|Wani Clan|ja|和珥氏|12=draft}}). According to this genealogy, Oshihime is listed as the daughter of {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命|1=Prince Ameoshitarashi|12=draft}}, with an alternate name of Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto. She is recorded as Emperor Kōan's empress, while her brother is identified as Wani Hikoshihito no Mikoto. The accuracy of this record remains disputed. {{ill|Higo Ryūkai|ja|比護隆界|12=draft}} claimed that these genealogies were fabricated in later periods.Higo Ryūkai, "Formation and Reliability of Clan Genealogies: On the 'Wani Clan Genealogy' from Suruga Sengen Shrine," *Japanese Ancient History Essays*, 1988. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == Sources == * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō (historian)|ja|坂本太郎|12=draft}} and Hirano Kunio, *Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Comprehensive Overview of the Imperial Family Lineage* (Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha) * {{ill|Ōta Ryō|ja|太田亮|12=draft}}, *Dictionary of Surnames and Genealogies* (Kadokawa Shoten)== See Also == * {{ill|Eight Unhistorical Monarchs|ja|欠史八代|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka Period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=draft}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{citation needed|Oshihime}} {{translated page|ja|押媛|version=104216939|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:Wani Clan]] [[Category:Japanese Empresses]] [[Category:Empress Dowagers of Japan]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Figures]] [[Category:Female Imperial Family Members Before the Kofun Period]] [[Category:Birth and Death Dates Unknown]] [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Oshihime]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Oshihime]] [[sn:Oshihime]] [[ja:押媛]] [[en:Oshihime]] [[fa:%D8%A7%D9%88%D8%B4%DB%8C %D9%87%DB%8C%D9%85%D9%87]]

She is said to be the daughter of {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命|1=Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=draft}}, making her both the niece and consort of Emperor Kōan. However, in the first supplementary text of the Nihon Shoki regarding Emperor Kōan, the empress is identified as Nagahime, daughter of Shiki Agatanushi Hae. In the second supplementary text, the empress is named Isosaka-hime, daughter of Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko. [[File:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|thumb|right|400px|Family tree of emperors (1st–10th) [[Media:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|View SVG (browser support required)]]]] == References == The {{ill|Fuji clan|ja|富士氏|qq=draft}}, who served as grand priests of {{ill|Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha|ja|富士山本宮浅間大社|qq=draft}}, trace their lineage to the Wani clan and preserved the Wani genealogy (see {{ill|Wani Clan|ja|和珥氏|qq=draft}}). According to this genealogy, Oshihime is listed as the daughter of {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命|1=Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=draft}}, with an alternate name of Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto. She is recorded as Emperor Kōan's empress, while her brother is identified as Wani Hikoshihito no Mikoto. The accuracy of this record remains disputed. {{ill|Higo Ryūkai|ja|比護隆界|qq=draft}} claimed that these genealogies were fabricated in later periods.Higo Ryūkai, "Formation and Reliability of Clan Genealogies: On the 'Wani Clan Genealogy' from Suruga Sengen Shrine," *Japanese Ancient History Essays*, 1988. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == Sources == * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō (historian)|ja|坂本太郎|qq=draft}} and Hirano Kunio, *Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Comprehensive Overview of the Imperial Family Lineage* (Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha) * {{ill|Ōta Ryō|ja|太田亮|qq=draft}}, *Dictionary of Surnames and Genealogies* (Kadokawa Shoten)== See Also == * {{ill|Eight Unhistorical Monarchs|ja|欠史八代|qq=draft}} * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka Period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|qq=draft}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} [[Category:translated pages without meta categories]]\n{{translated page|ja|押媛|version=104216939|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Wani Clan]] [[Category:Japanese Empresses]] [[Category:Empress Dowagers of Japan]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Figures]] [[Category:Female Imperial Family Members Before the Kofun Period]] [[Category:Birth and Death Dates Unknown]] [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Oshihime]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Oshihime]] [[sn:Oshihime]] [[ja:押媛]] [[en:Oshihime]] [[fa:%D8%A7%D9%88%D8%B4%DB%8C %D9%87%DB%8C%D9%85%D9%87]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox royalty | name = Oshihime (押媛) | image = | caption = | reign = | era_name_reign = | imperial_consort = Emperor Kōan (孝安天皇) February 14, 26th year of Emperor Kōan | empress_dowager = Emperor Kōrei (孝霊天皇) January 12, 1st year of Emperor Kōrei | birth_date = | birth_place = | death_date = | death_place = | burial_place = | clan = {{ill|Wani Clan|ja|和邇氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani Clan|qq=}} | father = {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命|1=Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametarashihikokunioshihito|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=}} | spouse1 = {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kōan|qq=}} | children = {{ill|Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|ja|大吉備諸進命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kōrei|qq=}} | other_titles = Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto (忍鹿比売命)
Oshihime no Mikoto (押媛命) | other_theories = Daughter of {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi Hae|ja|磯城県主葉江|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Agatanushi Hae|qq=}}, named Nagahime
Daughter of {{ill|Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko|ja|十市県主五十坂彦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko|qq=}}, named Isosaka-hime }} '''Oshihime''' (押媛) is a figure from ancient Japan. == Overview == Alternate titles include '''Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto''' (忍鹿比売命) and Oshihime no Mikoto (押媛命). She is featured in historical texts such as the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}}, {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}, and {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai Kuji Hongi|qq=}}. Oshihime is described as the consort of {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kōan|qq=}}, mother to {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kōrei|qq=}} and {{ill|Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|ja|大吉備諸進命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|qq=}}. She is said to be the daughter of {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命|1=Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametarashihikokunioshihito|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=}}, making her both the niece and consort of Emperor Kōan. However, in the first supplementary text of the Nihon Shoki regarding Emperor Kōan, the empress is identified as Nagahime, daughter of Shiki Agatanushi Hae. In the second supplementary text, the empress is named Isosaka-hime, daughter of Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko. [[File:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|thumb|right|400px|Family tree of emperors (1st–10th) [[Media:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|View SVG (browser support required)]]]] == References == The {{ill|Fuji clan|ja|富士氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuji clan|qq=}}, who served as grand priests of {{ill|Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha|ja|富士山本宮浅間大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha|qq=}}, trace their lineage to the Wani clan and preserved the Wani genealogy (see {{ill|Wani Clan|ja|和珥氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani Clan|qq=}}). According to this genealogy, Oshihime is listed as the daughter of {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命|1=Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametarashihikokunioshihito|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=}}, with an alternate name of Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto. She is recorded as Emperor Kōan's empress, while her brother is identified as Wani Hikoshihito no Mikoto. The accuracy of this record remains disputed. {{ill|Higo Ryūkai|ja|比護隆界|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higo Ryūkai|qq=}} claimed that these genealogies were fabricated in later periods.Higo Ryūkai, "Formation and Reliability of Clan Genealogies: On the 'Wani Clan Genealogy' from Suruga Sengen Shrine," *Japanese Ancient History Essays*, 1988. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == Sources == * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō (historian)|ja|坂本太郎|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Tarō (historian)|qq=}} and Hirano Kunio, *Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Comprehensive Overview of the Imperial Family Lineage* (Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha) * {{ill|Ōta Ryō|ja|太田亮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta Ryō|qq=}}, *Dictionary of Surnames and Genealogies* (Kadokawa Shoten)== See Also == * {{ill|Eight Unhistorical Monarchs|ja|欠史八代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Unhistorical Monarchs|qq=}} * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka Period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of pre-Asuka Period figures|qq=}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|押媛|version=104216939|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Wani Clan]] [[Category:Japanese Empresses]] [[Category:Empress Dowagers of Japan]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Figures]] [[Category:Female Imperial Family Members Before the Kofun Period]] [[Category:Birth and Death Dates Unknown]] [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Oshihime]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Oshihime]] [[sn:Oshihime]] [[ja:押媛]] [[en:Oshihime]] [[fa:%D8%A7%D9%88%D8%B4%DB%8C %D9%87%DB%8C%D9%85%D9%87]]

[[File:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|thumb|right|400px|Family tree of emperors (1st–10th) [[Media:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|View SVG (browser support required)]]]] == References == The {{ill|Fuji clan|ja|富士氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuji clan|qq=}}, who served as grand priests of {{ill|Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha|ja|富士山本宮浅間大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha|qq=}}, trace their lineage to the Wani clan and preserved the Wani genealogy (see {{ill|Wani Clan|ja|和珥氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani Clan|qq=}}). According to this genealogy, Oshihime is listed as the daughter of {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命|1=Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametarashihikokunioshihito|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=}}, with an alternate name of Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto. She is recorded as Emperor Kōan's empress, while her brother is identified as Wani Hikoshihito no Mikoto. The accuracy of this record remains disputed. {{ill|Higo Ryūkai|ja|比護隆界|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higo Ryūkai|qq=}} claimed that these genealogies were fabricated in later periods.Higo Ryūkai, "Formation and Reliability of Clan Genealogies: On the 'Wani Clan Genealogy' from Suruga Sengen Shrine," *Japanese Ancient History Essays*, 1988. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == Sources == * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō (historian)|ja|坂本太郎|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Tarō (historian)|qq=}} and Hirano Kunio, *Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Comprehensive Overview of the Imperial Family Lineage* (Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha) * {{ill|Ōta Ryō|ja|太田亮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta Ryō|qq=}}, *Dictionary of Surnames and Genealogies* (Kadokawa Shoten)== See Also == * {{ill|Eight Unhistorical Monarchs|ja|欠史八代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Unhistorical Monarchs|qq=}} * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka Period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of pre-Asuka Period figures|qq=}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|押媛|version=104216939|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Empress Dowagers of Japan]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Figures]] [[Category:Female Imperial Family Members Before the Kofun Period]] [[Category:Birth and Death Dates Unknown]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Oshihime]] [[sn:Oshihime]] [[ja:押媛]] [[en:Oshihime]] [[fa:%D8%A7%D9%88%D8%B4%DB%8C %D9%87%DB%8C%D9%85%D9%87]]

[[File:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|thumb|right|400px|Family tree of emperors (1st–10th) [[Media:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|View SVG (browser support required)]]]] == References == The {{ill|Fuji clan|ja|富士氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuji clan|qq=}}, who served as grand priests of [[:en:Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha|Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha]], trace their lineage to the Wani clan and preserved the Wani genealogy (see [[:en:Wani clan|Wani Clan]]). According to this genealogy, Oshihime is listed as the daughter of {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命|1=Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametarashihikokunioshihito|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=}}, with an alternate name of Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto. She is recorded as Emperor Kōan's empress, while her brother is identified as Wani Hikoshihito no Mikoto. The accuracy of this record remains disputed. {{ill|Higo Ryūkai|ja|比護隆界|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higo Ryūkai|qq=}} claimed that these genealogies were fabricated in later periods.Higo Ryūkai, "Formation and Reliability of Clan Genealogies: On the 'Wani Clan Genealogy' from Suruga Sengen Shrine," *Japanese Ancient History Essays*, 1988. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == Sources == * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō (historian)|ja|坂本太郎|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Tarō (historian)|qq=}} and Hirano Kunio, *Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Comprehensive Overview of the Imperial Family Lineage* (Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha) * {{ill|Ōta Ryō|ja|太田亮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta Ryō|qq=}}, *Dictionary of Surnames and Genealogies* (Kadokawa Shoten)== See Also == * {{ill|Eight Unhistorical Monarchs|ja|欠史八代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Unhistorical Monarchs|qq=}} * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka Period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of pre-Asuka Period figures|qq=}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|押媛|version=104216939|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Empress Dowagers of Japan]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Figures]] [[Category:Female Imperial Family Members Before the Kofun Period]] [[Category:Birth and Death Dates Unknown]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Oshihime]] [[sn:Oshihime]] [[ja:押媛]] [[en:Oshihime]] [[fa:%D8%A7%D9%88%D8%B4%DB%8C %D9%87%DB%8C%D9%85%D9%87]] [[Category:古墳時代以前の女性皇族]] [[Category:和珥氏]] [[Category:日本の皇太后]]

In the second supplementary text, the empress is named Isosaka-hime, daughter of Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko. [[File:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|thumb|right|400px|Family tree of emperors (1st–10th) [[Media:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|View SVG (browser support required)]]]] == References == The {{ill|Fuji clan|ja|富士氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuji clan|qq=}}, who served as grand priests of [[:en:Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha|Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha]], trace their lineage to the Wani clan and preserved the Wani genealogy (see [[:en:Wani clan|Wani Clan]]). According to this genealogy, Oshihime is listed as the daughter of {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命|1=Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametarashihikokunioshihito|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=}}, with an alternate name of Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto. She is recorded as Emperor Kōan's empress, while her brother is identified as Wani Hikoshihito no Mikoto. The accuracy of this record remains disputed. {{ill|Higo Ryūkai|ja|比護隆界|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higo Ryūkai|qq=}} claimed that these genealogies were fabricated in later periods.Higo Ryūkai, "Formation and Reliability of Clan Genealogies: On the 'Wani Clan Genealogy' from Suruga Sengen Shrine," *Japanese Ancient History Essays*, 1988. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == Sources == * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō (historian)|ja|坂本太郎|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Tarō (historian)|qq=}} and Hirano Kunio, *Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Comprehensive Overview of the Imperial Family Lineage* (Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha) * {{ill|Ōta Ryō|ja|太田亮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta Ryō|qq=}}, *Dictionary of Surnames and Genealogies* (Kadokawa Shoten)== See Also == * {{ill|Eight Unhistorical Monarchs|ja|欠史八代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Unhistorical Monarchs|qq=}} * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka Period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of pre-Asuka Period figures|qq=}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|押媛|version=104216939|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Empress Dowagers of Japan]] [[Category:Female Imperial Family Members Before the Kofun Period]] [[Category:Birth and Death Dates Unknown]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Oshihime]] [[sn:Oshihime]] [[ja:押媛]] [[fa:%D8%A7%D9%88%D8%B4%DB%8C %D9%87%DB%8C%D9%85%D9%87]] [[Category:古墳時代以前の女性皇族]] [[Category:和珥氏]] [[Category:日本の皇太后]] [[Category:生没年不詳]] [[fa:اوشی هیمه]] [[ko:오시히메]] [[zh:押媛]]

In the second supplementary text, the empress is named Isosaka-hime, daughter of Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko. The accuracy of this record remains disputed. {{ill|Higo Ryūkai|ja|比護隆界|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higo Ryūkai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} claimed that these genealogies were Daughter of {{ill|Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko|ja|十市県主五十坂彦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, named Isosaka-hime }} '''Oshihime''' (押媛) is a figure from ancient Japan. == Overview == Alternate titles include '''Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto''' (忍鹿比売命) and Oshihime no Mikoto (押媛命). She is featured in historical texts such as the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], and [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]. Oshihime is described as the consort of [[:en:Emperor Kōan|Emperor Kōan]], mother to [[:en:Emperor Kōrei|Emperor Kōrei]] and {{ill|Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|ja|大吉備諸進命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. She is said to be the daughter of {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命|1=Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametarashihikokunioshihito|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=}}, making her both the niece and consort of Emperor Kōan. However, in the first supplementary text of the Nihon Shoki regarding Emperor Kōan, the empress is identified as Nagahime, daughter of Shiki Agatanushi Hae. In the second supplementary text, the empress is named Isosaka-hime, daughter of Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko. [[File:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|thumb|right|400px|Family tree of emperors (1st–10th) [[Media:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|View SVG (browser support required)]]]] == References == The {{ill|Fuji clan|ja|富士氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuji clan|qq=}}, who served as grand priests of [[:en:Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha|Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha]], trace their lineage to the Wani clan and preserved the Wani genealogy (see [[:en:Wani clan|Wani Clan]]). According to this genealogy, Oshihime is listed as the daughter of {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命|1=Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametarashihikokunioshihito|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=}}, with an alternate name of Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto. She is recorded as Emperor Kōan's empress, while her brother is identified as Wani Hikoshihito no Mikoto. The accuracy of this record remains disputed. {{ill|Higo Ryūkai|ja|比護隆界|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higo Ryūkai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} claimed that these genealogies were fabricated in later periods.Higo Ryūkai, "Formation and Reliability of Clan Genealogies: On the 'Wani Clan Genealogy' from Suruga Sengen Shrine," *Japanese Ancient History Essays*, 1988. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == Sources == * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō (historian)|ja|坂本太郎|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Tarō (historian)|qq=}} and Hirano Kunio, *Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Comprehensive Overview of the Imperial Family Lineage* (Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha) * {{ill|Ōta Ryō|ja|太田亮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta Ryō|qq=}}, *Dictionary of Surnames and Genealogies* (Kadokawa Shoten)== See Also == * {{ill|Eight Unhistorical Monarchs|ja|欠史八代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Unhistorical Monarchs|qq=}} * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka Period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of pre-Asuka Period figures|qq=}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|押媛|version=104216939|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Oshihime]] [[sn:Oshihime]] [[ja:押媛]] [[fa:%D8%A7%D9%88%D8%B4%DB%8C %D9%87%DB%8C%D9%85%D9%87]] [[Category:古墳時代以前の女性皇族]] [[Category:和珥氏]] [[Category:日本の皇太后]] [[Category:生没年不詳]] [[fa:اوشی هیمه]] [[ko:오시히메]] [[zh:押媛]]

revision.comment: Merge [[Category:古墳時代以前の女性皇族]] into [[Category:Female Imperial Family Members before the Kofun Period]] via bot
revision.text:
{{Infobox royalty | name = Oshihime (押媛) | image = | caption = | reign = | era_name_reign = | imperial_consort = Emperor Kōan (孝安天皇) February 14, 26th year of Emperor Kōan | empress_dowager = Emperor Kōrei (孝霊天皇) January 12, 1st year of Emperor Kōrei | birth_date = | birth_place = | death_date = | death_place = | burial_place = | clan = {{ill|Wani Clan|ja|和邇氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 和珥氏}} | father = {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命|1=Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametarashihikokunioshihito|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=}} | spouse1 = [[:en:Emperor Kōan|Emperor Kōan]] | children = {{ill|Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|ja|大吉備諸進命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
[[:en:Emperor Kōrei|Emperor Kōrei]] | other_titles = Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto (忍鹿比売命)
Oshihime no Mikoto (押媛命) | other_theories = Daughter of {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi Hae|ja|磯城県主葉江|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Agatanushi Hae|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, named Nagahime
Daughter of {{ill|Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko|ja|十市県主五十坂彦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, named Isosaka-hime }} '''Oshihime''' (押媛) is a figure from ancient Japan. == Overview == Alternate titles include '''Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto''' (忍鹿比売命) and Oshihime no Mikoto (押媛命). She is featured in historical texts such as the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], and [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]. Oshihime is described as the consort of [[:en:Emperor Kōan|Emperor Kōan]], mother to [[:en:Emperor Kōrei|Emperor Kōrei]] and {{ill|Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|ja|大吉備諸進命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. She is said to be the daughter of {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命|1=Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametarashihikokunioshihito|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=}}, making her both the niece and consort of Emperor Kōan. However, in the first supplementary text of the Nihon Shoki regarding Emperor Kōan, the empress is identified as Nagahime, daughter of Shiki Agatanushi Hae. In the second supplementary text, the empress is named Isosaka-hime, daughter of Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko. [[File:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|thumb|right|400px|Family tree of emperors (1st–10th) [[Media:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|View SVG (browser support required)]]]] == References == The {{ill|Fuji clan|ja|富士氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuji clan|qq=}}, who served as grand priests of [[:en:Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha|Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha]], trace their lineage to the Wani clan and preserved the Wani genealogy (see [[:en:Wani clan|Wani Clan]]). According to this genealogy, Oshihime is listed as the daughter of {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命|1=Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametarashihikokunioshihito|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=}}, with an alternate name of Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto. She is recorded as Emperor Kōan's empress, while her brother is identified as Wani Hikoshihito no Mikoto. The accuracy of this record remains disputed. {{ill|Higo Ryūkai|ja|比護隆界|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higo Ryūkai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} claimed that these genealogies were fabricated in later periods.Higo Ryūkai, "Formation and Reliability of Clan Genealogies: On the 'Wani Clan Genealogy' from Suruga Sengen Shrine," *Japanese Ancient History Essays*, 1988. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == Sources == * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō (historian)|ja|坂本太郎|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Tarō (historian)|qq=}} and Hirano Kunio, *Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Comprehensive Overview of the Imperial Family Lineage* (Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha) * {{ill|Ōta Ryō|ja|太田亮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta Ryō|qq=}}, *Dictionary of Surnames and Genealogies* (Kadokawa Shoten)== See Also == * {{ill|Eight Unhistorical Monarchs|ja|欠史八代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Unhistorical Monarchs|qq=}} * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka Period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of pre-Asuka Period figures|qq=}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|押媛|version=104216939|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Oshihime]] [[sn:Oshihime]] [[ja:押媛]] [[fa:%D8%A7%D9%88%D8%B4%DB%8C %D9%87%DB%8C%D9%85%D9%87]] [[Category:Female Imperial Family Members before the Kofun Period]] [[Category:Wani Clan]] [[Category:Empress Dowagers of Japan]] [[Category:Year of Birth and Death unknown]] [[fa:اوشی هیمه]] [[ko:오시히메]] [[zh:押媛]]

Oshihime no Mikoto (押媛命) | other_theories = Daughter of {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi Hae|ja|磯城県主葉江|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Agatanushi Hae|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, named Nagahime
Daughter of {{ill|Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko|ja|十市県主五十坂彦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, named Isosaka-hime }} '''Oshihime''' (押媛) is a figure from ancient Japan. == Overview == Alternate titles include '''Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto''' (忍鹿比売命) and Oshihime no Mikoto (押媛命). She is featured in historical texts such as the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], and [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]. Oshihime is described as the consort of [[:en:Emperor Kōan|Emperor Kōan]], mother to [[:en:Emperor Kōrei|Emperor Kōrei]] and {{ill|Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|ja|大吉備諸進命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. She is said to be the daughter of {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命|1=Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametarashihikokunioshihito|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=}}, making her both the niece and consort of Emperor Kōan. However, in the first supplementary text of the Nihon Shoki regarding Emperor Kōan, the empress is identified as Nagahime, daughter of Shiki Agatanushi Hae. In the second supplementary text, the empress is named Isosaka-hime, daughter of Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko. [[File:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|thumb|right|400px|Family tree of emperors (1st–10th) [[Media:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|View SVG (browser support required)]]]] == References == The {{ill|Fuji clan|ja|富士氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuji clan|qq=}}, who served as grand priests of [[:en:Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha|Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha]], trace their lineage to the Wani clan and preserved the Wani genealogy (see [[:en:Wani clan|Wani Clan]]). According to this genealogy, Oshihime is listed as the daughter of {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命|1=Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametarashihikokunioshihito|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=}}, with an alternate name of Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto. She is recorded as Emperor Kōan's empress, while her brother is identified as Wani Hikoshihito no Mikoto. The accuracy of this record remains disputed. {{ill|Higo Ryūkai|ja|比護隆界|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higo Ryūkai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} claimed that these genealogies were fabricated in later periods.Higo Ryūkai, "Formation and Reliability of Clan Genealogies: On the 'Wani Clan Genealogy' from Suruga Sengen Shrine," *Japanese Ancient History Essays*, 1988. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == Sources == * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō (historian)|ja|坂本太郎|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Tarō (historian)|qq=}} and Hirano Kunio, *Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Comprehensive Overview of the Imperial Family Lineage* (Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha) * {{ill|Ōta Ryō|ja|太田亮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta Ryō|qq=}}, *Dictionary of Surnames and Genealogies* (Kadokawa Shoten)== See Also == * {{ill|Eight Unhistorical Monarchs|ja|欠史八代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Unhistorical Monarchs|qq=}} * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka Period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of pre-Asuka Period figures|qq=}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|押媛|version=104216939|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Oshihime]] [[sn:Oshihime]] [[ja:押媛]] [[fa:%D8%A7%D9%88%D8%B4%DB%8C %D9%87%DB%8C%D9%85%D9%87]] [[Category:Female Imperial Family Members before the Kofun Period]] [[Category:Wani Clan]] [[Category:Empress Dowagers of Japan]] [[Category:Year of Birth and Death unknown]] [[fa:اوشی هیمه]] [[ko:오시히메]] [[zh:押媛]]

The accuracy of this record remains disputed. {{ill|Higo Ryūkai|ja|比護隆界|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higo Ryūkai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} claimed that these genealogies were fabricated in later periods.Higo Ryūkai, "Formation and Reliability of Clan Genealogies: On the 'Wani Clan Genealogy' from Suruga Sengen Shrine," *Japanese Ancient History Essays*, 1988. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == Sources == * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō (historian)|ja|坂本太郎|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Tarō (historian)|qq=}} and Hirano Kunio, *Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Comprehensive Overview of the Imperial Family Lineage* (Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha) * {{ill|Ōta Ryō|ja|太田亮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta Ryō|qq=}}, *Dictionary of Surnames and Genealogies* (Kadokawa Shoten)== See Also == * {{ill|Eight Unhistorical Monarchs|ja|欠史八代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Unhistorical Monarchs|qq=}} * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka Period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of pre-Asuka Period figures|qq=}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|押媛|version=104216939|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Oshihime]] [[sn:Oshihime]] [[ja:押媛]] [[fa:%D8%A7%D9%88%D8%B4%DB%8C %D9%87%DB%8C%D9%85%D9%87]] [[Category:Female Imperial Family Members before the Kofun Period]] [[Category:Wani Clan]] [[Category:Empress Dowagers of Japan]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]][[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]] [[fa:اوشی هیمه]] [[ko:오시히메]] [[zh:押媛]]

{{Infobox person|gender={{ill|female|qid=Q6581072}}|title={{ill|Empress of Japan|qid=Q595038}}|spouse={{ill|Emperor Kōan|qid=Q312821}}|children={{ill|Kōrei|qid=Q313041}}|father={{ill|Ametarashihiko kunioshihito no mikoto|qid=Q11443593}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11076797]] Wikidata name: Empress Oshihime [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox royalty | name = Oshihime (押媛) | image = | caption = | reign = | era_name_reign = | imperial_consort = Emperor Kōan (孝安天皇) February 14, 26th year of Emperor Kōan | empress_dowager = Emperor Kōrei (孝霊天皇) January 12, 1st year of Emperor Kōrei | birth_date = | birth_place = | death_date = | death_place = | burial_place = | clan = {{ill|Wani Clan|ja|和邇氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 和珥氏|1=Wani clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417873}} | father = {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命|1=Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametarashihikokunioshihito|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=}} | spouse1 = [[:en:Emperor Kōan|Emperor Kōan]] | children = {{ill|Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|ja|大吉備諸進命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
[[:en:Emperor Kōrei|Emperor Kōrei]] | other_titles = Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto (忍鹿比売命)
Oshihime no Mikoto (押媛命) | other_theories = Daughter of {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi Hae|ja|磯城県主葉江|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Agatanushi Hae|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, named Nagahime
Daughter of {{ill|Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko|ja|十市県主五十坂彦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, named Isosaka-hime }} '''Oshihime''' (押媛) is a figure from ancient Japan. == Overview == Alternate titles include '''Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto''' (忍鹿比売命) and Oshihime no Mikoto (押媛命). She is featured in historical texts such as the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], and [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]. Oshihime is described as the consort of [[:en:Emperor Kōan|Emperor Kōan]], mother to [[:en:Emperor Kōrei|Emperor Kōrei]] and {{ill|Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|ja|大吉備諸進命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. She is said to be the daughter of {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命|1=Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametarashihikokunioshihito|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=}}, making her both the niece and consort of Emperor Kōan. However, in the first supplementary text of the Nihon Shoki regarding Emperor Kōan, the empress is identified as Nagahime, daughter of Shiki Agatanushi Hae. In the second supplementary text, the empress is named Isosaka-hime, daughter of Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko. [[File:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|thumb|right|400px|Family tree of emperors (1st–10th) [[Media:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|View SVG (browser support required)]]]] == References == The {{ill|Fuji clan|ja|富士氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuji clan|qq=|1=Fuji clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11456091}}, who served as grand priests of [[:en:Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha|Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha]], trace their lineage to the Wani clan and preserved the Wani genealogy (see [[:en:Wani clan|Wani Clan]]). According to this genealogy, Oshihime is listed as the daughter of {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命|1=Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametarashihikokunioshihito|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=}}, with an alternate name of Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto. She is recorded as Emperor Kōan's empress, while her brother is identified as Wani Hikoshihito no Mikoto. The accuracy of this record remains disputed. {{ill|Higo Ryūkai|ja|比護隆界|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higo Ryūkai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} claimed that these genealogies were fabricated in later periods.Higo Ryūkai, "Formation and Reliability of Clan Genealogies: On the 'Wani Clan Genealogy' from Suruga Sengen Shrine," *Japanese Ancient History Essays*, 1988. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == Sources == * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō (historian)|ja|坂本太郎|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Tarō (historian)|qq=|1=Sakamoto Tarō (historian)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425251}} and Hirano Kunio, *Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Comprehensive Overview of the Imperial Family Lineage* (Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha) * {{ill|Ōta Ryō|ja|太田亮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta Ryō|qq=|1=Akira Ōta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13099367}}, *Dictionary of Surnames and Genealogies* (Kadokawa Shoten)== See Also == * {{ill|Eight Unhistorical Monarchs|ja|欠史八代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Unhistorical Monarchs|qq=|1=Kesshi Hachidai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q838196}} * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka Period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of pre-Asuka Period figures|qq=|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|押媛|version=104216939|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] }} revision.text:
{{Infobox person|gender={{ill|female|qid=Q6581072}}|title={{ill|Empress of Japan|qid=Q595038}}|spouse={{ill|Emperor Kōan|qid=Q312821}}|children={{ill|Kōrei|qid=Q313041}}|father={{ill|Ametarashihiko kunioshihito no mikoto|qid=Q11443593}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11076797]] Wikidata name: Empress Oshihime [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox royalty | name = Oshihime (押媛) | image = | caption = | reign = | era_name_reign = | imperial_consort = Emperor Kōan (孝安天皇) February 14, 26th year of Emperor Kōan | empress_dowager = Emperor Kōrei (孝霊天皇) January 12, 1st year of Emperor Kōrei | birth_date = | birth_place = | death_date = | death_place = | burial_place = | clan = {{ill|Wani Clan|ja|和邇氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 和珥氏|1=Wani clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417873}} | father = {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命|1=Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametarashihikokunioshihito|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=}} | spouse1 = [[:en:Emperor Kōan|Emperor Kōan]] | children = {{ill|Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|ja|大吉備諸進命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
[[:en:Emperor Kōrei|Emperor Kōrei]] | other_titles = Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto (忍鹿比売命)
Oshihime no Mikoto (押媛命) | other_theories = Daughter of {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi Hae|ja|磯城県主葉江|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Agatanushi Hae|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, named Nagahime
Daughter of {{ill|Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko|ja|十市県主五十坂彦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, named Isosaka-hime }} '''Oshihime''' (押媛) is a figure from ancient Japan. == Overview == Alternate titles include '''Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto''' (忍鹿比売命) and Oshihime no Mikoto (押媛命). She is featured in historical texts such as the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], and [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]. Oshihime is described as the consort of [[:en:Emperor Kōan|Emperor Kōan]], mother to [[:en:Emperor Kōrei|Emperor Kōrei]] and {{ill|Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|ja|大吉備諸進命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. She is said to be the daughter of {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命|1=Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametarashihikokunioshihito|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=}}, making her both the niece and consort of Emperor Kōan. However, in the first supplementary text of the Nihon Shoki regarding Emperor Kōan, the empress is identified as Nagahime, daughter of Shiki Agatanushi Hae. In the second supplementary text, the empress is named Isosaka-hime, daughter of Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko. [[File:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|thumb|right|400px|Family tree of emperors (1st–10th) [[Media:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|View SVG (browser support required)]]]] == References == The {{ill|Fuji clan|ja|富士氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuji clan|qq=|1=Fuji clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11456091}}, who served as grand priests of [[:en:Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha|Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha]], trace their lineage to the Wani clan and preserved the Wani genealogy (see [[:en:Wani clan|Wani Clan]]). According to this genealogy, Oshihime is listed as the daughter of {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命|1=Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametarashihikokunioshihito|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=}}, with an alternate name of Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto. She is recorded as Emperor Kōan's empress, while her brother is identified as Wani Hikoshihito no Mikoto. The accuracy of this record remains disputed. {{ill|Higo Ryūkai|ja|比護隆界|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higo Ryūkai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} claimed that these genealogies were fabricated in later periods.Higo Ryūkai, "Formation and Reliability of Clan Genealogies: On the 'Wani Clan Genealogy' from Suruga Sengen Shrine," *Japanese Ancient History Essays*, 1988. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == Sources == * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō (historian)|ja|坂本太郎|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Tarō (historian)|qq=|1=Sakamoto Tarō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425251}} and Hirano Kunio, *Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Comprehensive Overview of the Imperial Family Lineage* (Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha) * {{ill|Ōta Ryō|ja|太田亮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta Ryō|qq=|1=Akira Ōta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13099367}}, *Dictionary of Surnames and Genealogies* (Kadokawa Shoten)== See Also == * {{ill|Eight Unhistorical Monarchs|ja|欠史八代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Unhistorical Monarchs|qq=|1=Kesshi Hachidai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q838196}} * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka Period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of pre-Asuka Period figures|qq=|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|押媛|version=104216939|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] }} [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Oshihime]] [[sn:Oshihime]] [[ja:押媛]] [[fa:%D8%A7%D9%88%D8%B4%DB%8C %D9%87%DB%8C%D9%85%D9%87]] [[Category:Female Imperial Family Members before the Kofun Period]] [[Category:Wani Clan]] [[Category:Empress Dowagers of Japan]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]][[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]] [[fa:اوشی هیمه]] [[ko:오시히메]] [[zh:押媛]]

[[:en:Emperor Kōrei|Emperor Kōrei]] | other_titles = Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto (忍鹿比売命)
Oshihime no Mikoto (押媛命) | other_theories = Daughter of {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi Hae|ja|磯城県主葉江|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Agatanushi Hae|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, named Nagahime
Daughter of {{ill|Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko|ja|十市県主五十坂彦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, named Isosaka-hime }} '''Oshihime''' (押媛) is a figure from ancient Japan. == Overview == Alternate titles include '''Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto''' (忍鹿比売命) and Oshihime no Mikoto (押媛命). She is featured in historical texts such as the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], and [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]. Oshihime is described as the consort of [[:en:Emperor Kōan|Emperor Kōan]], mother to [[:en:Emperor Kōrei|Emperor Kōrei]] and {{ill|Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|ja|大吉備諸進命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. She is said to be the daughter of {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命|1=Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametarashihikokunioshihito|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=}}, making her both the niece and consort of Emperor Kōan. However, in the first supplementary text of the Nihon Shoki regarding Emperor Kōan, the empress is identified as Nagahime, daughter of Shiki Agatanushi Hae. In the second supplementary text, the empress is named Isosaka-hime, daughter of Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko. [[File:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|thumb|right|400px|Family tree of emperors (1st–10th) [[Media:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|View SVG (browser support required)]]]] == References == The {{ill|Fuji clan|ja|富士氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuji clan|qq=|1=Fuji clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11456091}}, who served as grand priests of [[:en:Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha|Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha]], trace their lineage to the Wani clan and preserved the Wani genealogy (see [[:en:Wani clan|Wani Clan]]). According to this genealogy, Oshihime is listed as the daughter of {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命|1=Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametarashihikokunioshihito|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=}}, with an alternate name of Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto. She is recorded as Emperor Kōan's empress, while her brother is identified as Wani Hikoshihito no Mikoto. The accuracy of this record remains disputed. {{ill|Higo Ryūkai|ja|比護隆界|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higo Ryūkai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} claimed that these genealogies were fabricated in later periods.Higo Ryūkai, "Formation and Reliability of Clan Genealogies: On the 'Wani Clan Genealogy' from Suruga Sengen Shrine," *Japanese Ancient History Essays*, 1988. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == Sources == * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō (historian)|ja|坂本太郎|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Tarō (historian)|qq=|1=Sakamoto Tarō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425251}} and Hirano Kunio, *Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Comprehensive Overview of the Imperial Family Lineage* (Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha) * {{ill|Ōta Ryō|ja|太田亮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta Ryō|qq=|1=Akira Ōta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13099367}}, *Dictionary of Surnames and Genealogies* (Kadokawa Shoten)== See Also == * {{ill|Eight Unhistorical Monarchs|ja|欠史八代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Unhistorical Monarchs|qq=|1=Kesshi Hachidai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q838196}} * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka Period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of pre-Asuka Period figures|qq=|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|押媛|version=104216939|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] }} Daughter of {{ill|Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko|ja|十市県主五十坂彦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, named Isosaka-hime }} '''Oshihime''' (押媛) is a figure from ancient Japan. == Overview == Alternate titles include '''Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto''' (忍鹿比売命) and Oshihime no Mikoto (押媛命). She is featured in historical texts such as the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], and [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]. Oshihime is described as the consort of [[:en:Emperor Kōan|Emperor Kōan]], mother to [[:en:Emperor Kōrei|Emperor Kōrei]] and {{ill|Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|ja|大吉備諸進命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. She is said to be the daughter of {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命|1=Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametarashihikokunioshihito|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=}}, making her both the niece and consort of Emperor Kōan. However, in the first supplementary text of the Nihon Shoki regarding Emperor Kōan, the empress is identified as Nagahime, daughter of Shiki Agatanushi Hae. In the second supplementary text, the empress is named Isosaka-hime, daughter of Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko. [[File:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|thumb|right|400px|Family tree of emperors (1st–10th) [[Media:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|View SVG (browser support required)]]]] == References == The {{ill|Fuji clan|ja|富士氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuji clan|qq=|1=Fuji clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11456091}}, who served as grand priests of [[:en:Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha|Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha]], trace their lineage to the Wani clan and preserved the Wani genealogy (see [[:en:Wani clan|Wani Clan]]). According to this genealogy, Oshihime is listed as the daughter of {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命|1=Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametarashihikokunioshihito|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=}}, with an alternate name of Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto. She is recorded as Emperor Kōan's empress, while her brother is identified as Wani Hikoshihito no Mikoto. The accuracy of this record remains disputed. {{ill|Higo Ryūkai|ja|比護隆界|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higo Ryūkai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} claimed that these genealogies were fabricated in later periods.Higo Ryūkai, "Formation and Reliability of Clan Genealogies: On the 'Wani Clan Genealogy' from Suruga Sengen Shrine," *Japanese Ancient History Essays*, 1988. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == Sources == * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō (historian)|ja|坂本太郎|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Tarō (historian)|qq=|1=Sakamoto [[File:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|thumb|right|400px|Family tree of emperors (1st–10th) [[Media:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|View SVG (browser support required)]]]] == References == The {{ill|Fuji clan|ja|富士氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuji clan|qq=|1=Fuji clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11456091}}, who served as grand priests of [[:en:Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha|Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha]], trace their lineage to the Wani clan and preserved the Wani genealogy (see [[:en:Wani clan|Wani Clan]]). According to this genealogy, Oshihime is listed as the daughter of {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命|1=Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametarashihikokunioshihito|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=}}, with an alternate name of Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto. She is recorded as Emperor Kōan's empress, while her brother is identified as Wani Hikoshihito no Mikoto. The accuracy of this record remains disputed. {{ill|Higo Ryūkai|ja|比護隆界|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higo Ryūkai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} claimed that these genealogies were fabricated in later periods.Higo Ryūkai, "Formation and Reliability of Clan Genealogies: On the 'Wani Clan Genealogy' from Suruga Sengen Shrine," *Japanese Ancient History Essays*, 1988. == Genealogy [[:en:Emperor Kōrei|Emperor Kōrei]] | other_titles = Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto (忍鹿比売命)
Oshihime no Mikoto (押媛命) | other_theories = Daughter of {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi Hae|ja|磯城県主葉江|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Agatanushi Hae|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, named Nagahime
Daughter of {{ill|Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko|ja|十市県主五十坂彦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, named Isosaka-hime }} '''Oshihime''' (押媛) is a figure from ancient Japan. == Overview == Alternate titles include '''Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto''' (忍鹿比売命) and Oshihime no Mikoto (押媛命). She is featured in historical texts such as the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], and [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]. Oshihime is described as the consort of [[:en:Emperor Kōan|Emperor Kōan]], mother to [[:en:Emperor Kōrei|Emperor Kōrei]] and {{ill|Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|ja|大吉備諸進命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. She is said to be the daughter of {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命|1=Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametarashihikokunioshihito|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=}}, making her both the niece and consort of Emperor Kōan. However, in the first supplementary text of the Nihon Shoki regarding Emperor Kōan, the empress is identified as Nagahime, daughter of Shiki Agatanushi Hae. In the second supplementary text, the empress is named Isosaka-hime, daughter of Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko. [[File:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|thumb|right|400px|Family tree of emperors (1st–10th) [[Media:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|View SVG (browser support required)]]]] == References == The {{ill|Fuji clan|ja|富士氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuji clan|1=Fuji clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11456091}}, who served as grand priests of [[:en:Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha|Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha]], trace their lineage to the Wani clan and preserved the Wani genealogy (see [[:en:Wani clan|Wani Clan]]). According to this genealogy, Oshihime is listed as the daughter of {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命|1=Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametarashihikokunioshihito|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=}}, with an alternate name of Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto. She is recorded as Emperor Kōan's empress, while her brother is identified as Wani Hikoshihito no Mikoto. The accuracy of this record remains disputed. {{ill|Higo Ryūkai|ja|比護隆界|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higo Ryūkai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} claimed that these genealogies were fabricated in later periods.Higo Ryūkai, "Formation and Reliability of Clan Genealogies: On the 'Wani Clan Genealogy' from Suruga Sengen Shrine," *Japanese Ancient History Essays*, 1988. == Genealogy [[en:Oshihime]][[fa:اوشی هیمه]][[ja:押媛]][[ko:오시히메]][[zh:押媛]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|title={{ill|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038}}|spouse={{ill|Emperor Kōan|WD=Q312821}}|children={{ill|Kōrei|WD=Q313041}}|father={{ill|Ametarashihiko kunioshihito no mikoto|WD=Q11443593}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11076797]] Wikidata name: Empress Oshihime [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox royalty | name = Oshihime (押媛) | image = | caption = | reign = | era_name_reign = | imperial_consort = Emperor Kōan (孝安天皇) February 14, 26th year of Emperor Kōan | empress_dowager = Emperor Kōrei (孝霊天皇) January 12, 1st year of Emperor Kōrei | birth_date = | birth_place = | death_date = | death_place = | burial_place = | clan = {{ill|Wani Clan|ja|和邇氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani Clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 和珥氏|1=Wani clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417873}} | father = {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命|1=Prince Ameoshitarashi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametarashihikokunioshihito|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=}} | spouse1 = [[:en:Emperor Kōan|Emperor Kōan]] | children = {{ill|Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|ja|大吉備諸進命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
[[:en:Emperor Kōrei|Emperor Kōrei]] | other_titles = Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto (忍鹿比売命)
Oshihime no Mikoto (押媛命) | other_theories = Daughter of {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi Hae|ja|磯城県主葉江|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Agatanushi Hae|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, named Nagahime
Daughter of {{ill|Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko|ja|十市県主五十坂彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, named Isosaka-hime }} '''Oshihime''' (押媛) is a figure from ancient Japan. == Overview == Alternate titles include '''Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto''' (忍鹿比売命) and Oshihime no Mikoto (押媛命). She is featured in historical texts such as the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], and [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]. Oshihime is described as the consort of [[:en:Emperor Kōan|Emperor Kōan]], mother to [[:en:Emperor Kōrei|Emperor Kōrei]] and {{ill|Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|ja|大吉備諸進命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. She is said to be the daughter of {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命|1=Prince Ameoshitarashi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametarashihikokunioshihito|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=}}, making her both the niece and consort of Emperor Kōan. However, in the first supplementary text of the Nihon Shoki regarding Emperor Kōan, the empress is identified as Nagahime, daughter of Shiki Agatanushi Hae. In the second supplementary text, the empress is named Isosaka-hime, daughter of Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko. [[File:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|thumb|right|400px|Family tree of emperors (1st–10th) [[Media:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|View SVG (browser support required)]]]] == References == The {{ill|Fuji clan|ja|富士氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuji clan|1=Fuji clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11456091}}, who served as grand priests of [[:en:Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha|Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha]], trace their lineage to the Wani clan and preserved the Wani genealogy (see [[:en:Wani clan|Wani Clan]]). According to this genealogy, Oshihime is listed as the daughter of {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命|1=Prince Ameoshitarashi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametarashihikokunioshihito|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=}}, with an alternate name of Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto. She is recorded as Emperor Kōan's empress, while her brother is identified as Wani Hikoshihito no Mikoto. The accuracy of this record remains disputed. {{ill|Higo Ryūkai|ja|比護隆界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higo Ryūkai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} claimed that these genealogies were fabricated in later periods.Higo Ryūkai, "Formation and Reliability of Clan Genealogies: On the 'Wani Clan Genealogy' from Suruga Sengen Shrine," *Japanese Ancient History Essays*, 1988. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == Sources == * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō (historian)|ja|坂本太郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Tarō (historian)|1=Sakamoto Tarō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425251}} and Hirano Kunio, *Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Comprehensive Overview of the Imperial Family Lineage* (Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha) * {{ill|Ōta Ryō|ja|太田亮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta Ryō|1=Akira Ōta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13099367}}, *Dictionary of Surnames and Genealogies* (Kadokawa Shoten)== See Also == * {{ill|Eight Unhistorical Monarchs|ja|欠史八代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Unhistorical Monarchs|1=Kesshi Hachidai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q838196}} * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka Period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of pre-Asuka Period figures|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|押媛|version=104216939|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] }} [[ja:押媛]] [[fa:%D8%A7%D9%88%D8%B4%DB%8C %D9%87%DB%8C%D9%85%D9%87]] [[Category:Female Imperial Family Members before the Kofun Period]] [[Category:Wani Clan]] [[Category:Empress Dowagers of Japan]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]][[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]] [[fa:اوشی هیمه]] [[ko:오시히메]] [[zh:押媛]] {{wikidata link|Q11076797}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[:en:Emperor Kōrei|Emperor Kōrei]] | other_titles = Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto (忍鹿比売命)
Oshihime no Mikoto (押媛命) | other_theories = Daughter of {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi Hae|ja|磯城県主葉江|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Agatanushi Hae|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, named Nagahime

She is featured in historical texts such as the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], and [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]. Oshihime is described as the consort of [[:en:Emperor Kōan|Emperor Kōan]], mother to [[:en:Emperor Kōrei|Emperor Kōrei]] and {{ill|Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|ja|大吉備諸進命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. She is said to be the daughter of {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命|1=Prince Ameoshitarashi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametarashihikokunioshihito|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=}}, making her both the niece and consort of Emperor Kōan. However, in the first supplementary text of the Nihon Shoki regarding Emperor Kōan, the empress is identified as Nagahime, daughter of Shiki Agatanushi Hae. In the second supplementary text, the empress is named Isosaka-hime, daughter of Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko. [[File:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|thumb|right|400px|Family tree of emperors (1st–10th) [[Media:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|View SVG (browser support required)]]]] == References == The {{ill|Fuji clan|ja|富士氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuji clan|1=Fuji clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11456091}}, who served as grand priests of [[:en:Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha|Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha]], trace their lineage to the Wani clan and preserved the Wani genealogy (see [[:en:Wani clan|Wani Clan]]). According to this genealogy, Oshihime is listed as the daughter of {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命|1=Prince Ameoshitarashi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametarashihikokunioshihito|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=}}, with an alternate name of Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto. She is recorded as Emperor Kōan's empress, while her brother is identified as Wani Hikoshihito no Mikoto. The accuracy of this record remains disputed. {{ill|Higo Ryūkai|ja|比護隆界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higo Ryūkai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} claimed that these genealogies were fabricated in later periods.Higo Ryūkai, "Formation and Reliability of Clan Genealogies: On the 'Wani Clan Genealogy' from Suruga Sengen Shrine," *Japanese Ancient History Essays*, 1988. == Genealogy == [[en:Oshihime]][[fa:اوشی هیمه]][[ja:押媛]][[ko:오시히메]][[zh:押媛]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|title={{ill|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038}}|spouse={{ill|Emperor Kōan|WD=Q312821}}|children={{ill|Kōrei|WD=Q313041}}|father={{ill|Ametarashihiko kunioshihito no mikoto|WD=Q11443593}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11076797]] Wikidata name: Empress Oshihime [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox royalty | name = Oshihime (押媛) | image = | caption = | reign = | era_name_reign = | imperial_consort = Emperor Kōan (孝安天皇) February 14, 26th year of Emperor Kōan | empress_dowager = Emperor Kōrei (孝霊天皇) January 12, 1st year of Emperor Kōrei | birth_date = | birth_place = | death_date = | death_place = | burial_place = | clan = {{ill|Wani Clan|ja|和邇氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani Clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 和珥氏|1=Wani clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417873}} | father = {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命|1=Prince Ameoshitarashi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametarashihikokunioshihito|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=}} | spouse1 = [[:en:Emperor Kōan|Emperor Kōan]] | children = {{ill|Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|ja|大吉備諸進命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
[[:en:Emperor Kōrei|Emperor Kōrei]] | other_titles = Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto (忍鹿比売命)
Oshihime no Mikoto (押媛命) | other_theories = Daughter of {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi Hae|ja|磯城県主葉江|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Agatanushi Hae|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, named Nagahime
Daughter of {{ill|Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko|ja|十市県主五十坂彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, named Isosaka-hime }} '''Oshihime''' (押媛) is a figure from ancient Japan. == Overview == Alternate titles include '''Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto''' (忍鹿比売命) and Oshihime no Mikoto (押媛命). She is featured in historical texts such as the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], and [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]. Oshihime is described as the consort of [[:en:Emperor Kōan|Emperor Kōan]], mother to [[:en:Emperor Kōrei|Emperor Kōrei]] and {{ill|Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|ja|大吉備諸進命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. She is said to be the daughter of {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命|1=Prince Ameoshitarashi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametarashihikokunioshihito|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=}}, making her both the niece and consort of Emperor Kōan. However, in the first supplementary text of the Nihon Shoki regarding Emperor Kōan, the empress is identified as Nagahime, daughter of Shiki Agatanushi Hae. In the second supplementary text, the empress is named Isosaka-hime, daughter of Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko. [[File:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|thumb|right|400px|Family tree of emperors (1st–10th) [[Media:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|View SVG (browser support required)]]]] == References == The {{ill|Fuji clan|ja|富士氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuji clan|1=Fuji clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11456091}}, who served as grand priests of [[:en:Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha|Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha]], trace their lineage to the Wani clan and preserved the Wani genealogy (see [[:en:Wani clan|Wani Clan]]). According to this genealogy, Oshihime is listed as the daughter of {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命|1=Prince Ameoshitarashi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametarashihikokunioshihito|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=}}, with an alternate name of Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto. She is recorded as Emperor Kōan's empress, while her brother is identified as Wani Hikoshihito no Mikoto. The accuracy of this record remains disputed. {{ill|Higo Ryūkai|ja|比護隆界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higo Ryūkai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} claimed that these genealogies were fabricated in later periods.Higo Ryūkai, "Formation and Reliability of Clan Genealogies: On the 'Wani Clan Genealogy' from Suruga Sengen Shrine," *Japanese Ancient History Essays*, 1988. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == Sources == * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō (historian)|ja|坂本太郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Tarō (historian)|1=Sakamoto Tarō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425251}} and Hirano Kunio, *Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Comprehensive Overview of the Imperial Family Lineage* (Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha) * {{ill|Ōta Ryō|ja|太田亮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta Ryō|1=Akira Ōta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13099367}}, *Dictionary of Surnames and Genealogies* (Kadokawa Shoten)== See Also == * {{ill|Eight Unhistorical Monarchs|ja|欠史八代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Unhistorical Monarchs|1=Kesshi Hachidai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q838196}} * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka Period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of pre-Asuka Period figures|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|押媛|version=104216939|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] }} [[ja:押媛]] [[fa:%D8%A7%D9%88%D8%B4%DB%8C %D9%87%DB%8C%D9%85%D9%87]] [[Category:Female Imperial Family Members before the Kofun Period]] [[Category:Wani Clan]] [[Category:Empress Dowagers of Japan]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]][[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]] [[fa:اوشی هیمه]] [[ko:오시히메]] [[zh:押媛]] {{wikidata Daughter of {{ill|Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko|ja|十市県主五十坂彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, named Isosaka-hime }} '''Oshihime''' (押媛) is a figure from ancient Japan. == Overview == Alternate titles include '''Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto''' (忍鹿比売命) and Oshihime no Mikoto (押媛命). She is featured in historical texts such as the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], and [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]. Oshihime is described as the consort of [[:en:Emperor Kōan|Emperor Kōan]], mother to [[:en:Emperor Kōrei|Emperor Kōrei]] and {{ill|Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|ja|大吉備諸進命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. She is said to be the daughter of {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命|1=Prince Ameoshitarashi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametarashihikokunioshihito|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=}}, making her both the niece and consort of Emperor Kōan. However, in the first supplementary text of the Nihon Shoki regarding Emperor Kōan, the empress is identified as Nagahime, daughter of Shiki Agatanushi Hae. In the second supplementary text, the empress is named Isosaka-hime, daughter of Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko. [[File:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|thumb|right|400px|Family tree of emperors (1st–10th) [[Media:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|View SVG (browser support required)]]]] == References == The {{ill|Fuji clan|ja|富士氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuji clan|1=Fuji clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11456091}}, who served as grand priests of [[:en:Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha|Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha]], trace their lineage to the Wani clan and preserved the Wani genealogy (see [[:en:Wani clan|Wani Clan]]). According to this genealogy, Oshihime is listed as the daughter of {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命|1=Prince Ameoshitarashi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametarashihikokunioshihito|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=}}, with an alternate name of Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto. She is recorded as Emperor Kōan's empress, while her brother is identified as Wani Hikoshihito no Mikoto. The accuracy of this record remains disputed. {{ill|Higo Ryūkai|ja|比護隆界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higo Ryūkai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} claimed that these genealogies were fabricated in later periods.Higo Ryūkai, "Formation and Reliability of Clan Genealogies: On the 'Wani Clan Genealogy' from Suruga Sengen Shrine," *Japanese Ancient History Essays*, 1988. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == Sources == * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō (historian)|ja|坂本太郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Tarō (historian)|1=Sakamoto Tarō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425251}} and Hirano Kunio, *Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Comprehensive Overview of the Imperial Family Lineage* (Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha) * {{ill|Ōta Ryō|ja|太田亮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta Ryō|1=Akira Ōta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13099367}}, *Dictionary of Surnames and Genealogies* (Kadokawa Shoten)== See Also == *
