In the second supplementary text, the empress is named Isosaka-hime, daughter of Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko. [[File:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|thumb|right|400px|Family tree of emperors (1st–10th) [[Media:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|View SVG (browser support required)]]]] == References == The {{ill|Fuji clan|ja|富士氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuji clan|1=Fuji clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11456091}}, who served as grand priests of [[:en:Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha|Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha]], trace their lineage to the Wani clan and preserved the Wani genealogy (see [[:en:Wani clan|Wani Clan]]). According to this genealogy, Oshihime is listed as the daughter of {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命|1=Prince Ameoshitarashi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametarashihikokunioshihito|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=}}, with an alternate name of Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto. She is recorded as Emperor Kōan's empress, while her brother is identified as Wani Hikoshihito no Mikoto. The accuracy of this record remains disputed. {{ill|Higo Ryūkai|ja|比護隆界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higo Ryūkai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} claimed that these genealogies were fabricated in later periods.Higo Ryūkai, "Formation and Reliability of Clan Genealogies: On the 'Wani Clan Genealogy' from Suruga Sengen Shrine," *Japanese Ancient History Essays*, 1988. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == Sources == * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō (historian)|ja|坂本太郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Tarō (historian)|1=Sakamoto Tarō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425251}} and Hirano Kunio, *Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Comprehensive Overview of the Imperial Family Lineage* (Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha) * {{ill|Ōta Ryō|ja|太田亮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta Ryō|1=Akira Ōta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13099367}}, *Dictionary of Surnames and Genealogies* (Kadokawa Shoten)== See Also == * Oshihime no Mikoto (押媛命) | other_theories = Daughter of {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi Hae|ja|磯城県主葉江|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Agatanushi Hae|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, named Nagahime
Daughter of {{ill|Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko|ja|十市県主五十坂彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, named Isosaka-hime }} '''Oshihime''' (押媛) is a figure from ancient Japan. == Overview == Alternate titles include '''Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto''' (忍鹿比売命) and Oshihime no Mikoto (押媛命). She is featured in historical texts such as the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], and [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]. Oshihime is described as the consort of [[:en:Emperor Kōan|Emperor Kōan]], mother to [[:en:Emperor Kōrei|Emperor Kōrei]] and {{ill|Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|ja|大吉備諸進命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. She is said to be the daughter of {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命|1=Prince Ameoshitarashi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametarashihikokunioshihito|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=}}, making her both the niece and consort of Emperor Kōan. However, in the first supplementary text of the Nihon Shoki regarding Emperor Kōan, the empress is identified as Nagahime, daughter of Shiki Agatanushi Hae. In the second supplementary text, the empress is named Isosaka-hime, daughter of Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko. She is recorded as Emperor Kōan's empress, while her brother is identified as Wani Hikoshihito no Mikoto. The accuracy of this record remains disputed. {{ill|Higo Ryūkai|ja|比護隆界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higo Ryūkai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} claimed that these genealogies were fabricated in later periods.Higo Ryūkai, "Formation and Reliability of Clan Genealogies: On the 'Wani Clan Genealogy' from Suruga Sengen Shrine," *Japanese Ancient History Essays*, 1988. == Genealogy == later periods.Higo Ryūkai, "Formation and Reliability of Clan Genealogies: On the 'Wani Clan Genealogy' from Suruga Sengen Shrine," *Japanese Ancient History Essays*, 1988. == Genealogy == revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
[[en:Oshihime]][[fa:اوشی هیمه]][[ja:押媛]][[ko:오시히메]][[zh:押媛]] [[en:Oshihime]][[fa:اوشی هیمه]][[ja:押媛]][[ko:오시히메]][[zh:押媛]] [[en:Oshihime]][[fa:اوشی هیمه]][[ja:押媛]][[ko:오시히메]][[zh:押媛]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|title={{ill|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038}}|spouse={{ill|Emperor Kōan|WD=Q312821}}|children={{ill|Kōrei|WD=Q313041}}|father={{ill|Ametarashihiko kunioshihito no mikoto|WD=Q11443593}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11076797]] Wikidata name: Empress Oshihime [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox royalty | name = Oshihime (押媛) | image = | caption = | reign = | era_name_reign = | imperial_consort = Emperor Kōan (孝安天皇) February 14, 26th year of Emperor Kōan | empress_dowager = Emperor Kōrei (孝霊天皇) January 12, 1st year of Emperor Kōrei | birth_date = | birth_place = | death_date = | death_place = | burial_place = | clan = {{ill|Wani Clan|ja|和邇氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani Clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 和珥氏|1=Wani clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417873}} | father = {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命|1=Prince Ameoshitarashi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametarashihikokunioshihito|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=}} | spouse1 = {{ill|Emperor Kōan|en|Emperor Kōan|WD=Q312821|lt=Emperor Kōan}} | children = {{ill|Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|ja|大吉備諸進命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
{{ill|Emperor Kōrei|en|Emperor Kōrei|WD=Q313041|lt=Emperor Kōrei}} | other_titles = Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto (忍鹿比売命)
Oshihime no Mikoto (押媛命) | other_theories = Daughter of {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi Hae|ja|磯城県主葉江|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Agatanushi Hae|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, named Nagahime
Daughter of {{ill|Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko|ja|十市県主五十坂彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, named Isosaka-hime }} '''Oshihime''' (押媛) is a figure from ancient Japan. == Overview == Alternate titles include '''Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto''' (忍鹿比売命) and Oshihime no Mikoto (押媛命). She is featured in historical texts such as the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}, {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, and {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi}}. Oshihime is described as the consort of {{ill|Emperor Kōan|en|Emperor Kōan|WD=Q312821|lt=Emperor Kōan}}, mother to {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|en|Emperor Kōrei|WD=Q313041|lt=Emperor Kōrei}} and {{ill|Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|ja|大吉備諸進命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. She is said to be the daughter of {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命|1=Prince Ameoshitarashi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametarashihikokunioshihito|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=}}, making her both the niece and consort of Emperor Kōan. However, in the first supplementary text of the Nihon Shoki regarding Emperor Kōan, the empress is identified as Nagahime, daughter of Shiki Agatanushi Hae. In the second supplementary text, the empress is named Isosaka-hime, daughter of Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko. [[File:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|thumb|right|400px|Family tree of emperors (1st–10th) [[Media:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|View SVG (browser support required)]]]] == References == The {{ill|Fuji clan|ja|富士氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuji clan|1=Fuji clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11456091}}, who served as grand priests of {{ill|Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha|en|Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha|WD=Q653180|lt=Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha}}, trace their lineage to the Wani clan and preserved the Wani genealogy (see {{ill|Wani clan|en|Wani clan|WD=Q11417873|lt=Wani Clan}}). According to this genealogy, Oshihime is listed as the daughter of {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命|1=Prince Ameoshitarashi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametarashihikokunioshihito|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=}}, with an alternate name of Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto. She is recorded as Emperor Kōan's empress, while her brother is identified as Wani Hikoshihito no Mikoto. The accuracy of this record remains disputed. {{ill|Higo Ryūkai|ja|比護隆界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higo Ryūkai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} claimed that these genealogies were fabricated in later periods.Higo Ryūkai, "Formation and Reliability of Clan Genealogies: On the 'Wani Clan Genealogy' from Suruga Sengen Shrine," *Japanese Ancient History Essays*, 1988. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == Sources == * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō (historian)|ja|坂本太郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Tarō (historian)|1=Sakamoto Tarō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425251}} and Hirano Kunio, *Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Comprehensive Overview of the Imperial Family Lineage* (Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha) * {{ill|Ōta Ryō|ja|太田亮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta Ryō|1=Akira Ōta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13099367}}, *Dictionary of Surnames and Genealogies* (Kadokawa Shoten)== See Also == * {{ill|Eight Unhistorical Monarchs|ja|欠史八代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Unhistorical Monarchs|1=Kesshi Hachidai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q838196}} * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka Period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of pre-Asuka Period figures|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{translated page|ja|押媛|version=104216939|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:押媛]] [[fa:%D8%A7%D9%88%D8%B4%DB%8C %D9%87%DB%8C%D9%85%D9%87]] [[fa:اوشی هیمه]] revision.text:
[[en:Oshihime]][[fa:اوشی هیمه]][[ja:押媛]][[ko:오시히메]][[zh:押媛]] [[en:Oshihime]][[fa:اوشی هیمه]][[ja:押媛]][[ko:오시히메]][[zh:押媛]] [[en:Oshihime]][[fa:اوشی هیمه]][[ja:押媛]][[ko:오시히메]][[zh:押媛]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|title={{ill|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038}}|spouse={{ill|Emperor Kōan|WD=Q312821}}|children={{ill|Kōrei|WD=Q313041}}|father={{ill|Ametarashihiko kunioshihito no mikoto|WD=Q11443593}}}} {{Infobox royalty | name = Oshihime (押媛) | image = | caption = | reign = | era_name_reign = | imperial_consort = Emperor Kōan (孝安天皇) February 14, 26th year of Emperor Kōan | empress_dowager = Emperor Kōrei (孝霊天皇) January 12, 1st year of Emperor Kōrei | birth_date = | birth_place = | death_date = | death_place = | burial_place = | clan = {{ill|Wani Clan|ja|和邇氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani Clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 和珥氏|1=Wani clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417873}} | father = {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命|1=Prince Ameoshitarashi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametarashihikokunioshihito|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=}} | spouse1 = {{ill|Emperor Kōan|en|Emperor Kōan|WD=Q312821|lt=Emperor Kōan}} | children = {{ill|Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|ja|大吉備諸進命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
{{ill|Emperor Kōrei|en|Emperor Kōrei|WD=Q313041|lt=Emperor Kōrei}} | other_titles = Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto (忍鹿比売命)
Oshihime no Mikoto (押媛命) | other_theories = Daughter of {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi Hae|ja|磯城県主葉江|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Agatanushi Hae|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, named Nagahime
Daughter of {{ill|Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko|ja|十市県主五十坂彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, named Isosaka-hime }} '''Oshihime''' (押媛) is a figure from ancient Japan. == Overview == Alternate titles include '''Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto''' (忍鹿比売命) and Oshihime no Mikoto (押媛命). She is featured in historical texts such as the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}, {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, and {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi}}. Oshihime is described as the consort of {{ill|Emperor Kōan|en|Emperor Kōan|WD=Q312821|lt=Emperor Kōan}}, mother to {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|en|Emperor Kōrei|WD=Q313041|lt=Emperor Kōrei}} and {{ill|Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|ja|大吉備諸進命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. She is said to be the daughter of {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命|1=Prince Ameoshitarashi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametarashihikokunioshihito|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=}}, making her both the niece and consort of Emperor Kōan. However, in the first supplementary text of the Nihon Shoki regarding Emperor Kōan, the empress is identified as Nagahime, daughter of Shiki Agatanushi Hae. In the second supplementary text, the empress is named Isosaka-hime, daughter of Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko. [[File:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|thumb|right|400px|Family tree of emperors (1st–10th) [[Media:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|View SVG (browser support required)]]]] == References == The {{ill|Fuji clan|ja|富士氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuji clan|1=Fuji clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11456091}}, who served as grand priests of {{ill|Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha|en|Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha|WD=Q653180|lt=Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha}}, trace their lineage to the Wani clan and preserved the Wani genealogy (see {{ill|Wani clan|en|Wani clan|WD=Q11417873|lt=Wani Clan}}). According to this genealogy, Oshihime is listed as the daughter of {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命|1=Prince Ameoshitarashi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametarashihikokunioshihito|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=}}, with an alternate name of Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto. She is recorded as Emperor Kōan's empress, while her brother is identified as Wani Hikoshihito no Mikoto. The accuracy of this record remains disputed. {{ill|Higo Ryūkai|ja|比護隆界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higo Ryūkai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} claimed that these genealogies were fabricated in later periods.Higo Ryūkai, "Formation and Reliability of Clan Genealogies: On the 'Wani Clan Genealogy' from Suruga Sengen Shrine," *Japanese Ancient History Essays*, 1988. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == Sources == * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō (historian)|ja|坂本太郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Tarō (historian)|1=Sakamoto Tarō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425251}} and Hirano Kunio, *Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Comprehensive Overview of the Imperial Family Lineage* (Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha) * {{ill|Ōta Ryō|ja|太田亮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta Ryō|1=Akira Ōta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13099367}}, *Dictionary of Surnames and Genealogies* (Kadokawa Shoten)== See Also == * {{ill|Eight Unhistorical Monarchs|ja|欠史八代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Unhistorical Monarchs|1=Kesshi Hachidai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q838196}} * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka Period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of pre-Asuka Period figures|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{translated page|ja|押媛|version=104216939|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:押媛]] [[fa:%D8%A7%D9%88%D8%B4%DB%8C %D9%87%DB%8C%D9%85%D9%87]] [[fa:اوشی هیمه]] [[en:Oshihime]][[fa:اوشی هیمه]][[ja:押媛]][[ko:오시히메]][[zh:押媛]] [[en:Oshihime]][[fa:اوشی هیمه]][[ja:押媛]][[ko:오시히메]][[zh:押媛]] [[en:Oshihime]][[fa:اوشی هیمه]][[ja:押媛]][[ko:오시히메]][[zh:押媛]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072|1=Female (gender)}}|title={{ill|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038|1=Empress of Japan}}|spouse={{ill|Emperor Kōan|WD=Q312821|1=Emperor Kōan}}|children={{ill|Kōrei|WD=Q313041|1=Emperor Kōrei}}|father={{ill|Ametarashihiko kunioshihito no mikoto|WD=Q11443593|1=Prince Ametarashihiko}}}} {{Infobox royalty | name = Oshihime (押媛) | image = | caption = | reign = | era_name_reign = | imperial_consort = Emperor Kōan (孝安天皇) February 14, 26th year of Emperor Kōan | empress_dowager = Emperor Kōrei (孝霊天皇) January 12, 1st year of Emperor Kōrei | birth_date = | birth_place = | death_date = | death_place = | burial_place = | clan = {{ill|Wani Clan|ja|和邇氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani Clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 和珥氏|1=Wani clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417873}} | father = {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命|1=Prince Ameoshitarashi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametarashihikokunioshihito|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=}} | spouse1 = {{ill|Emperor Kōan|en|Emperor Kōan|WD=Q312821|lt=Emperor Kōan|1=Emperor Kōan}} | children = {{ill|Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|ja|大吉備諸進命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
{{ill|Emperor Kōrei|en|Emperor Kōrei|WD=Q313041|lt=Emperor Kōrei|1=Emperor Kōrei}} | other_titles = Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto (忍鹿比売命)
Oshihime no Mikoto (押媛命) | other_theories = Daughter of {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi Hae|ja|磯城県主葉江|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Agatanushi Hae|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, named Nagahime
Daughter of {{ill|Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko|ja|十市県主五十坂彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, named Isosaka-hime }} '''Oshihime''' (押媛) is a figure from ancient Japan. == Overview == Alternate titles include '''Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto''' (忍鹿比売命) and Oshihime no Mikoto (押媛命). She is featured in historical texts such as the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}, {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, and {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}}. Oshihime is described as the consort of {{ill|Emperor Kōan|en|Emperor Kōan|WD=Q312821|lt=Emperor Kōan|1=Emperor Kōan}}, mother to {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|en|Emperor Kōrei|WD=Q313041|lt=Emperor Kōrei|1=Emperor Kōrei}} and {{ill|Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|ja|大吉備諸進命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. She is said to be the daughter of {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命|1=Prince Ameoshitarashi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametarashihikokunioshihito|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=}}, making her both the niece and consort of Emperor Kōan. However, in the first supplementary text of the Nihon Shoki regarding Emperor Kōan, the empress is identified as Nagahime, daughter of Shiki Agatanushi Hae. In the second supplementary text, the empress is named Isosaka-hime, daughter of Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko. [[File:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|thumb|right|400px|Family tree of emperors (1st–10th) [[Media:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|View SVG (browser support required)]]]] == References == The {{ill|Fuji clan|ja|富士氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuji clan|1=Fuji clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11456091}}, who served as grand priests of {{ill|Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha|en|Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha|WD=Q653180|lt=Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha|1=Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha}}, trace their lineage to the Wani clan and preserved the Wani genealogy (see {{ill|Wani clan|en|Wani clan|WD=Q11417873|lt=Wani Clan|1=Wani clan}}). According to this genealogy, Oshihime is listed as the daughter of {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命|1=Prince Ameoshitarashi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametarashihikokunioshihito|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=}}, with an alternate name of Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto. She is recorded as Emperor Kōan's empress, while her brother is identified as Wani Hikoshihito no Mikoto. The accuracy of this record remains disputed. {{ill|Higo Ryūkai|ja|比護隆界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higo Ryūkai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} claimed that these genealogies were fabricated in later periods.Higo Ryūkai, "Formation and Reliability of Clan Genealogies: On the 'Wani Clan Genealogy' from Suruga Sengen Shrine," *Japanese Ancient History Essays*, 1988. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == Sources == * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō (historian)|ja|坂本太郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Tarō (historian)|1=Sakamoto Tarō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425251}} and Hirano Kunio, *Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Comprehensive Overview of the Imperial Family Lineage* (Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha) * {{ill|Ōta Ryō|ja|太田亮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta Ryō|1=Akira Ōta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13099367}}, *Dictionary of Surnames and Genealogies* (Kadokawa Shoten)== See Also == * {{ill|Eight Unhistorical Monarchs|ja|欠史八代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Unhistorical Monarchs|1=Kesshi Hachidai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q838196}} * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka Period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of pre-Asuka Period figures|1=list of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{translated page|ja|押媛|version=104216939|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:押媛]] [[fa:%D8%A7%D9%88%D8%B4%DB%8C %D9%87%DB%8C%D9%85%D9%87]] [[fa:اوشی هیمه]] revision.text:
{{Infobox person|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072|1=Female (gender)}}|title={{ill|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038|1=Empress of Japan}}|spouse={{ill|Emperor Kōan|WD=Q312821|1=Emperor Kōan}}|children={{ill|Kōrei|WD=Q313041|1=Emperor Kōrei}}|father={{ill|Ametarashihiko kunioshihito no mikoto|WD=Q11443593|1=Prince Ametarashihiko}}}} {{Infobox royalty | name = Oshihime (押媛) | image = | caption = | reign = | era_name_reign = | imperial_consort = Emperor Kōan (孝安天皇) February 14, 26th year of Emperor Kōan | empress_dowager = Emperor Kōrei (孝霊天皇) January 12, 1st year of Emperor Kōrei | birth_date = | birth_place = | death_date = | death_place = | burial_place = | clan = {{ill|Wani Clan|ja|和邇氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani Clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 和珥氏|1=Wani clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417873}} | father = {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命|1=Prince Ameoshitarashi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametarashihikokunioshihito|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=}} | spouse1 = {{ill|Emperor Kōan|en|Emperor Kōan|WD=Q312821|lt=Emperor Kōan|1=Emperor Kōan}} | children = {{ill|Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|ja|大吉備諸進命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
{{ill|Emperor Kōrei|en|Emperor Kōrei|WD=Q313041|lt=Emperor Kōrei|1=Emperor Kōrei}} | other_titles = Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto (忍鹿比売命)
Oshihime no Mikoto (押媛命) | other_theories = Daughter of {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi Hae|ja|磯城県主葉江|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Agatanushi Hae|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, named Nagahime
Daughter of {{ill|Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko|ja|十市県主五十坂彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, named Isosaka-hime }} '''Oshihime''' (押媛) is a figure from ancient Japan. == Overview == Alternate titles include '''Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto''' (忍鹿比売命) and Oshihime no Mikoto (押媛命). She is featured in historical texts such as the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}, {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, and {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}}. Oshihime is described as the consort of {{ill|Emperor Kōan|en|Emperor Kōan|WD=Q312821|lt=Emperor Kōan|1=Emperor Kōan}}, mother to {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|en|Emperor Kōrei|WD=Q313041|lt=Emperor Kōrei|1=Emperor Kōrei}} and {{ill|Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|ja|大吉備諸進命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkibimoronoshi-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. She is said to be the daughter of {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命|1=Prince Ameoshitarashi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametarashihikokunioshihito|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=}}, making her both the niece and consort of Emperor Kōan. However, in the first supplementary text of the Nihon Shoki regarding Emperor Kōan, the empress is identified as Nagahime, daughter of Shiki Agatanushi Hae. In the second supplementary text, the empress is named Isosaka-hime, daughter of Tōchi Agatanushi Isosaka-hiko. [[File:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|thumb|right|400px|Family tree of emperors (1st–10th) [[Media:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|View SVG (browser support required)]]]] == References == The {{ill|Fuji clan|ja|富士氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuji clan|1=Fuji clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11456091}}, who served as grand priests of {{ill|Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha|en|Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha|WD=Q653180|lt=Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha|1=Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha}}, trace their lineage to the Wani clan and preserved the Wani genealogy (see {{ill|Wani clan|en|Wani clan|WD=Q11417873|lt=Wani Clan|1=Wani clan}}). According to this genealogy, Oshihime is listed as the daughter of {{ill|Ametarashihikokunioshihito|ja|天足彦国押人命|1=Prince Ameoshitarashi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametarashihikokunioshihito|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ameoshitarashi|qq=}}, with an alternate name of Shinokahi-hime no Mikoto. She is recorded as Emperor Kōan's empress, while her brother is identified as Wani Hikoshihito no Mikoto. The accuracy of this record remains disputed. {{ill|Higo Ryūkai|ja|比護隆界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higo Ryūkai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} claimed that these genealogies were fabricated in later periods.Higo Ryūkai, "Formation and Reliability of Clan Genealogies: On the 'Wani Clan Genealogy' from Suruga Sengen Shrine," *Japanese Ancient History Essays*, 1988. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == Sources == * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō (historian)|ja|坂本太郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Tarō (historian)|1=Sakamoto Tarō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425251}} and Hirano Kunio, *Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Comprehensive Overview of the Imperial Family Lineage* (Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha) *
